Title: TCS employee's death: Two booked for abetment over colleague's 'suicide', company says it is ascertaining facts
Article: TCS said in its statement that it had taken note of the allegations against the two employees. Case against 3, incl. 2 TCS staff, for abetting suicide Note claims workplace humiliation and negative feedback TCS says it is investigating and supporting the bereaved family Did our AI summary help? Police have registered a case of alleged abetment to suicide against three persons including two employees of Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) following the death of a 48-year-old staffer of the IT firm, an official said on Friday. While no arrest has been made in the case, TCS said it had taken note of the allegations against its employees and was ascertaining the facts. The case was registered at the Bhosari police station in neighbouring Pimpri-Chinchwad city on a complaint filed by the deceased's son, a police official said. Amit Brahme, who worked at the Hinjawadi office of TCS, allegedly hanged himself at his residence in Bhosari on June 2. In his purported suicide note, he named a friend named Vinod Palicha for allegedly sending defamatory emails about him to the company. Related Stories TCS ties up with Anthropic, to roll out Claude AI access to 50,000 employees TCS will take strict action if any associate is found responsible: N Chandrasekaran on Nashik case TCS AI revenue nears $2.5 billion annualised; AI agents could match employee count: N Chandrasekaran The note also named female colleagues Archana and Shashwati, accusing them of repeatedly humiliating him in front of other colleagues, depriving him of favourable work assignments and assigning him projects outside his area of expertise. The two women reprimanded him over his performance, asked him to quit his job in the presence of co-workers and threatened to give adverse feedback, the suicide note claimed. "We have registered a case under section 108 (abetment of suicide) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and are conducting further investigation. No arrest has been made so far," the police official said. TCS said in its statement that it had taken note of the allegations against the two employees. "The wellbeing of our employees is of utmost importance to us and we are extending all support to the bereaved family. We have also taken note of the allegations against the two employees and are ascertaining the facts," it said. It remained committed to "fostering a workplace that is respectful, supportive, and inclusive for all our employees," the IT major said. Meanwhile, Nascent Information Technology Employees Senate (NITES), an association of IT workers, has written to Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis and sought inquiry into the case.

Title: TCS ties up with Anthropic, to roll out Claude AI access to 50,000 employees
Article: TCS and Anthropic will also jointly take AI solutions to market across sectors including financial services, healthcare, life sciences, public services, aviation, telecom and medtech TCS partners with Anthropic to boost enterprise AI adoption TCS to give 50,000 staff access to Anthropic's Claude AI models Partnership to deliver AI for finance, health, telecom sectors Did our AI summary help? Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) on Thursday announced a global strategic partnership with Anthropic, the company behind AI assistant Claude, to help businesses adopt and deploy artificial intelligence technologies at scale. In an exchange filing, TCS said it will establish a dedicated business unit focused on developing joint industry solutions, customer offerings and AI expertise around Anthropic's Claude family of models through early access to the technology. The partnership is aimed at helping enterprises move AI projects beyond the pilot stage by combining Anthropic's AI models with TCS' implementation, governance and industry expertise. "Enterprise AI value comes from understanding business context, orchestrating complex systems, and applying deep AI engineering talent. By combining Claude with our industry expertise, engineering rigor, and large-scale transformation capabilities, we will help customers move faster to production, especially in industries where trust, resilience, and regulatory discipline are critical. This partnership reflects TCS' broader strategy to help clients become perpetually adaptive enterprises by turning frontier AI into transformation at enterprise scale," said TCS CEO and MD K Krithivasan. As part of the collaboration, TCS will provide enterprise-wide access to Claude for 50,000 associates across engineering, finance, legal, marketing and sales functions. Related Stories TCS employee's death: Two booked for abetment over colleague's 'suicide', company says it is ascerta... SpaceX IPO will mint billions for a new Silicon Valley hierarchy The company said the internal deployment will help it gain first-hand operational experience while supporting AI-led transformation programmes for clients. TCS and Anthropic will also jointly take AI solutions to market across sectors including financial services, healthcare, life sciences, public services, aviation, telecom and medtech. Highlighting Anthropic's focus on India and enterprise adoption, Dario Amodei, Co-founder and CEO of Anthropic, said: "We built Claude to be safe, trusted, and helpful, particularly in contexts where accuracy matters most. This partnership deepens our commitment to India, our second-largest market, with TCS bringing Claude to enterprises and professionals across the region and globally, including 50,000 of its employees." The collaboration will also extend to TCS products and platforms. In the UK, Diligenta will deploy Claude to improve customer experience, while TCS iON will offer learning and certification programmes on Claude models to help build an AI-ready workforce. Calling the partnership a significant step towards enterprise reinvention, Tata Sons Chairman N Chandrasekaran said: "This partnership reflects our shared conviction that AI will be foundational and transformative for enterprises worldwide. By combining Anthropic's capabilities with Tata Group's scale, trusted relationships, and nation-building commitment, we will accelerate enterprise reinvention and equip India's youth with the skills to lead in the AI era." The development comes a day after Meta partnered with Reliance Industries to build its first AI-enabled data centre in India, underscoring the growing race among global technology companies to expand artificial intelligence infrastructure.

Title: No further downsizing planned; still need to attract the best talent: TCS Chairman N Chandrasekaran
Article: N Chandrasekaran, Chairman, TCS TCS rules out further layoffs, plans selective hiring ahead AI agents to match employee count, hiring rate to slow down AI revenue at TCS grew 22 percent, may reach 100 percent by 2030 Did our AI summary help? Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) won’t have any further workforce downsizing, though it will hire relatively fewer people as the IT services major looks to integrate AI agents into workflows, Chairman N Chandrasekaran said on June 9. Speaking at the company’s 31st Annual General Meeting (AGM), Chandrasekaran said, "There is no downsizing of staff planned.” For context, he was answering to shareholder concerns on whether there would be more layoffs after the company decided to restructure the workforce by eliminating 12,000 jobs last year . He added that instead the company is looking at a workforce models where there will be equal number of AI agents and human employees work together. “We want to have the right talent, we want to use AI agents. We still need to attract the best talent. Just that the rate of addition won’t be as it was,” Chandrasekaran said. Related Stories TCS ties up with Anthropic, to roll out Claude AI access to 50,000 employees TCS will take strict action if any associate is found responsible: N Chandrasekaran on Nashik case TCS AI revenue nears $2.5 billion annualised; AI agents could match employee count: N Chandrasekaran He explained, “If the HR department had a metric for their ability to hire large number of talent, that metric will go away. Metrics will change for every department on the basis of how do they attract best talent, how well they deploy AI agents, reskill and train existing people.” AI agents to match employee count The TCS chairman said he expects a future where the number of AI agents could match the company's workforce. "If the company has half a million employees, the day is not far when the company will have half a million AI agents," he said. Even as AI changes workforce requirements, Chandrasekaran argued that the technology represents the biggest opportunity the company has seen . He expects that the global IT industry, which is currently worth $1.6 trillion, will hit $3 trillion within the next 5-10 years, effectively doubling. AI is raising the ambition of what technology can deliver across every sector. Within TCS, Chandrasekaran shared that the company’s AI revenue has been growing consistently for the last four quarters. CQGR grew by 22% in that period. Last quarter AI revenues on annualised basis was close to $2.5 billion. He expects AI agents working within TCS to match its employee count soon, while AI component will account for almost 100 percent of its revenues by 2028-2030. With regards to this, TCS will start slowing down hiring and become more selective of its top talent.

Title: Far from being mortal threat, AI is the most significant opportunity yet for TCS: Chairman N Chandrasekaran
Article: N Chandrasekaran, Chairman, TCS AI seen as TCS's biggest opportunity, not a threat. TCS's AI revenue grew 22 percent over four quarters. AI may account for nearly all TCS revenue by 2028-2030. Did our AI summary help? Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) Chairman N Chandrasekaran on June 9 said that artificial intelligence (AI) is by far the biggest opportunity for the country’s largest IT services company, coming at a time when the IT industry is trying to win investors’ trust amid AI-led disruptions. He was speaking at the company’s 31st annual general meeting in Mumbai. Calming some of these fears, Chandrasekaran told shareholders, “Some say AI poses a fundamental threat to that (IT) model. I see it differently: far from being a mortal threat, AI is the most significant opportunity yet for TCS and enterprise IT.” He added that in late 2024, Agentic AI systems took over enterprise work such as writing code, testing software, running technology operations. This caused a serious question to crystallise: “if AI can do this work, what happens to an industry built around doing it?” “India's Nifty IT index fell more than a third. Globally, shares in all technology services and platform companies plummeted. And yet, margins have held. Revenues are up. The deal pipeline is stronger than ever. The business fundamentals have held,” he said. Related Stories TCS employee's death: Two booked for abetment over colleague's 'suicide', company says it is ascerta... LTM launches AI 1000 initiative to build talent pool of forward deployed engineers Who is Vik Bajaj? Meet the co-founder of Prometheus, Jeff Bezos's new AI startup What’s driving this optimism? According to Chandrasekaran, AI tools reduce the need for human input in the building and maintenance of software. But it is not merely a technology. “It is infrastructure - an infrastructure of intelligence.” “As the cost of intelligence falls, many more processes, decisions and interactions - in many more industries - will become candidates for AI-driven transformation. Globally, three-quarters of enterprises expect technology spend to increase over the next two years, largely thanks to AI. Our market is widening as organisations once unserved by IT services require our expertise for the first time,” Chandrasekaran said. He expects that the global IT industry, which is currently worth $1.6 trillion, will hit $3 trillion within the next 5-10 years, effectively doubling. AI is raising the ambition of what technology can deliver across every sector. Within TCS, Chandrasekaran shared that the company’s AI revenue has been growing consistently for the last four quarters. CQGR grew by 22% in that period. Last quarter AI revenues on annualised basis was close to $2.5 billion. He expects AI agents working within TCS to match its employee count soon, while AI component will account for almost 100 percent of its revenues by 2028-2030. With regards to this, TCS will start slowing down hiring and become more selective of its top talent.

Title: TCS launches new business unit to help enterprises build AI-native GCCs; appoints Soumen Roy to lead
Article: Representative image TCS launches new unit to help firms build AI-native GCCs Soumen Roy named TCS GVIC head, reporting to CEO Krithivasan Unit provides full GCC services, emphasizing AI transformation Did our AI summary help? Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), on June 8 announced the launch of a new dedicated business unit focused on helping enterprises establish and scale artificial intelligence (AI)-native Global Capability Centres (GCCs), amid growing demand from multinational companies looking to transform their offshore operations into innovation hubs. The company has launched the Global Value & Innovation Centres (GVIC) Business Unit, which will provide end-to-end services spanning GCC strategy, setup, operations and AI-led transformation. As part of the move, TCS has appointed Soumen Roy as Head of TCS GVIC. Roy, who most recently served as TCS Canada Country Head, will report directly to CEO and MD K Krithivasan. The announcement comes at a time when IT services companies are increasingly targeting growing GCC demand in pursuit of large agentic AI mandates. According to TCS, the new business unit combines the company's GCC consulting and execution capabilities with its AI, cloud, engineering and talent management expertise to help organisations build what it describes as AI-native GCCs. Related Stories TCS ties up with Anthropic, to roll out Claude AI access to 50,000 employees TCS will take strict action if any associate is found responsible: N Chandrasekaran on Nashik case TCS AI revenue nears $2.5 billion annualised; AI agents could match employee count: N Chandrasekaran The offering includes services across the entire GCC lifecycle, from advisory and setup to scaled operations and transformation. TCS said the model incorporates AI-enabled operating frameworks, cloud and data foundations, enterprise platforms, responsible AI governance mechanisms and talent development programmes. Krithivasan, said in a statement, “With TCS GVIC, we are bringing together TCS’ deep experience across the GCC lifecycle with our strengths in AI, engineering, talent, and operations to build high-impact, future-ready global centers that deliver sustained business value for clients.” The company said it currently works with more than 150 enterprises globally across various stages of the GCC lifecycle and aims to capitalise on rising investments in the sector as enterprises increasingly adopt AI-led operating models. The launch underscores the growing strategic importance of the GCC market for Indian IT services firms, many of which are expanding their consulting, transformation and managed services offerings around the rapidly growing GCC ecosystem.

Title: TCS bags 'multi-million euro' AI transformation mandate from Canada Life
Article: Representative image TCS wins multi-year tech transformation deal with Canada Life TCS to modernise and manage IT infrastructure across Europe Deal boosts TCS presence in European insurance technology sector Did our AI summary help? Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) has secured a multi-million euro, multi-year technology transformation and managed services contract from Canada Life, the IT services major said in a statement on June 8, without sharing the financial details. TCS will modernise and manage Canada Life's IT infrastructure services across its European businesses, including data centres, core infrastructure, end-user computing and software lifecycle management. Vinay Singhvi, Head of UK & Ireland at TCS, said the engagement underscores the company's growing focus on AI-led transformation. "By leveraging our deep domain expertise, we will help Canada Life achieve greater operational efficiency, enhance resilience, and create a more agile, responsive IT ecosystem to support their long-term growth,” he said. The deal with one of the world's leading life and pensions insurers comes days after the IT major lost parts of its long-running technology contract with Royal Bank of Canada (RBC). The company will deploy its artificial intelligence and digital capabilities to improve operational resilience, boost automation and enhance user experience, TCS said. Related Stories TCS employee's death: Two booked for abetment over colleague's 'suicide', company says it is ascerta... LTM launches AI 1000 initiative to build talent pool of forward deployed engineers Who is Vik Bajaj? Meet the co-founder of Prometheus, Jeff Bezos's new AI startup The deal also comes at a time when global insurers are increasingly investing in AI-led technology modernisation initiatives to streamline operations and improve customer engagement. The partnership will combine TCS' technology expertise with Canada Life's in-house teams to support the insurer's long-term technology transformation strategy. The programme is expected to deliver performance-driven IT services while helping the company scale technology operations and respond more quickly to changing business needs, it said. “TCS brings deep technical expertise, strong transformation capabilities and a collaborative approach that aligns well with our strategy. Together, we will deliver lasting improvements to the technology services and capabilities that support our business and the people who depend on it,” Caroline Dibbs, chief information & transformation officer, Europe said, As part of the contract, TCS will expand its infrastructure services talent pool across the UK, Ireland, the Isle of Man and Germany, investing in training, certification and career development programmes. The latest win strengthens TCS' position in the banking, financial services and insurance (BFSI) sector and reinforces its expansion strategy in the UK and Ireland, which the company sees as a key hub for delivering end-to-end transformation services to insurance clients across Europe.

Title: TCS partners Mistral to build custom AI models for enterprises, sets up dedicated CoE
Article: Representative image TCS partners with Mistral for enterprise AI solutions TCS to set up Centre of Excellence for Mistral Forge Tie-up targets BFSI, manufacturing, healthcare, public sector Did our AI summary help? India’s largest IT services company Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) on May 28 announced a strategic partnership with French artificial intelligence giant Mistral, becoming the first global systems integrator (GSI) partner for Mistral Forge, the company’s enterprise AI platform. As part of the collaboration, TCS will use Mistral Forge to help enterprises build custom AI models using their proprietary data and domain-specific knowledge, the company said in a statement. The Mumbai-headquartered IT major will also establish a dedicated Centre of Excellence (CoE) for Mistral to drive joint innovation, industry-specific AI solutions, project delivery and training initiatives. The CoE will additionally provide TCS with early access to Mistral’s beta AI models. The partnership comes as large IT firms deepen investments in generative AI capabilities amid rising demand from enterprises looking to move beyond pilot projects into large-scale deployment. TCS said the collaboration will initially focus on sectors including banking, financial services and insurance (BFSI), manufacturing, healthcare and the public sector, where enterprises are increasingly looking for AI systems tailored to regulatory and operational requirements. Related Stories TCS ties up with Anthropic, to roll out Claude AI access to 50,000 employees No further downsizing planned; still need to attract the best talent: TCS Chairman N Chandrasekaran Far from being mortal threat, AI is the most significant opportunity yet for TCS: Chairman N Chandra... Arthur Mensch, Chief Executive Officer and Co-Founder at Mistral, said, “TCS’ global scale and contextual industry knowledge make them an ideal partner for Mistral. Together, we are enabling enterprises worldwide to move from experimentation to AI deployment with systems that are open, production-ready and aligned with their strategic and operational requirements.” K Krithivasan, Chief Executive Officer & Managing Director at TCS said, “This partnership expands TCS’ AI ecosystem, uniquely positioning TCS to create a differentiated solution proposition for our clients. Together with Mistral, we will solve for specific industry challenges, regulatory requirements, and sovereign needs for our enterprise customers.” The move is aligned with TCS’ broader “Infrastructure to Intelligence” AI strategy, under which the company is investing across AI infrastructure, models, data, applications and platforms.

Title: TCS offers higher variable payouts of 60-80% for mid and senior employees in Q4
Article: Representative image TCS gives 60-80 percent variable pay to mid-senior staff in Q4 Office attendance affects eligibility for full variable payout Pay hikes of 1–13% tied to performance bands Did our AI summary help? Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) has maintained a higher quarterly variable payout for mid to senior-level employees for the second consecutive quarter in January-March. The IT services giant has given an average variable pay of 60-80 per cent in Q4, after nearly two years of keeping the payouts at 20-50 per cent through Q2 of FY26, sources aware of the matter told Moneycontrol . “Our quarterly variable pay for Q4 was released with April’s salary. I got between 60-80%,” one of the senior employees told Moneycontrol . However, employees with office attendance below the required threshold received lower payouts. One employee said many received around 50 percent of their variable pay as their office attendance stood at only about 60 percent. Since Q1FY25, the company has closely linked mandatory work-from-office requirements to eligibility for receiving quarterly variable allowance (QVA). Employees with less than 60 percent office attendance are not eligible for the quarterly bonus. As per the policy, TCS expects a minimum 85 percent attendance of employees in the office for them to be eligible to get full quarterly variable pay. Employees with 75-85 percent work from office attendance receive 75 percent of their variable pay while those with 60-75 percent attendance will only get 50 percent of their variable pay. Related Stories Stocks to Watch Today: Dabur India, Sagility, GNG Electronics, Lenskart Solutions, Hexagon Nutrition... TCS ties up with Anthropic, to roll out Claude AI access to 50,000 employees Infosys stock rating: CLSA bullish, sees $300-400 billion opportunity in GenAI; check target price TCS did not respond to Moneycontrol's queries on the quarterly variable pay at the time of publishing the story. Variable payout linked to performance Meanwhile, employees have seen lower performance-linked payouts over the past two years, as TCS trimmed variable pay despite office attendance compliance. “TCS has deducted anything between Rs 4-5 lakhs in the past two years as part of QVA,” said one of the employees. Previously, TCS had stated that, as a standard practice across quarters, variable pay is determined based on the business performance of an employee’s entire unit. TCS’ rival Infosys had reduced performance bonus payouts for the March quarter after disbursing its strongest variable pay in more than three years in the preceding quarter. The average bonus payout was around 70 percent, lower than the roughly 85 percent in the December quarter (Q3FY26), the IT services company’s best variable pay since the Covid period. TCS rolls out wage hikes TCS became the first IT company to start rolling out salary increments from April onwards for FY26. The company also released its new cost-to-company (CTC) structure, adjusting to the changes in the new Labour Codes. Moneycontrol had reported that the employees got an average salary hike of about 5 percent in the company’s latest appraisal cycle. TCS broadly categorises salary hikes based on performance bands such as A+, A, B and C, with top performers typically receiving higher increments and variable payouts. For the current cycle, employees in the top-rated A+ category said hikes ranged between 9 percent and 13 percent, while those in the A band largely reported increments of 5–9 percent. Employees in the B band mostly reported hikes in the 1–3.5 percent range, while many in the C band said their increments were negligible or even negative. Also read: TCS freezes promotion-linked ‘Wings’ assessment, upskilling amid AI-led workforce reset

Title: TCS partners with Rezolve Ai to enter agentic AI commerce market
Article: Representative image TCS, Rezolve Ai team up on agentic AI commerce solutions Retailers deploy Rezolve Ai’s brainpowa with TCS support Agentic commerce at TCS Pace Port innovation centers Did our AI summary help? Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) on May 12 announced partnership with AI-native commerce infrastructure provider Rezolve Ai to jointly develop and scale agentic AI-led commerce solutions for retailers globally. The partnership marks TCS’ entry into the emerging agentic commerce market, where artificial intelligence systems can autonomously manage and execute commerce-related workflows such as product discovery, conversational shopping and checkout processes. As part of the collaboration, TCS will help retail enterprises deploy Rezolve Ai’s proprietary commerce platform, brainpowa™, across enterprise-scale operations. The companies said the solutions are designed to enable AI-led customer experiences across conversational commerce, intelligent discovery and agentic checkout. Daniel M. Wagner, Chief Executive Officer, Rezolve Ai, said, “TCS brings extraordinary reach, deep enterprise trust, and global execution capability. Together, we are creating a clear pathway to take agentic commerce from platform innovation to enterprise‑scale deployment.” Shekar Krishnan, Head of Retail, UK and Europe, TCS, added, “This partnership is a key component of TCS’ strategy to create an integrated AI ecosystem bringing together in-house innovation with strategic partnerships to industrialize AI and deliver scalable, real-world outcomes for enterprises.” Related Stories TCS ties up with Anthropic, to roll out Claude AI access to 50,000 employees TCS will take strict action if any associate is found responsible: N Chandrasekaran on Nashik case TCS AI revenue nears $2.5 billion annualised; AI agents could match employee count: N Chandrasekaran The companies said retailers will also be able to test and experience the agentic commerce solutions through TCS’ Pace Port innovation centres globally. The move comes as Indian IT services firms deepen investments in artificial intelligence-led transformation offerings amid growing enterprise demand for automation and AI-native business applications. TCS has been increasingly expanding its AI ecosystem strategy through a mix of in-house platforms, data centres and partnerships with specialised AI companies. Rezolve Ai, listed on Nasdaq under the ticker RZLV, focuses on AI-powered commerce solutions aimed at improving customer engagement, operational efficiency and revenue growth for enterprises.

Title: TCS Nashik row: Inside the FIR trail and how a small unit drew outsized scrutiny
Article: TCS Nashik BPO survivor recounts harassment On April 16, about 50 Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) employees quietly arrived at the Nashik office in sweltering heat as anxiety rose over reports that the unit is shutting operations. For TCS, the Nashik unit is but a tiny operation of about 170 employees, given the IT giant's scale. Yet allegations of sexual harassment and workplace misconduct over four years, as detailed in police complaints now under investigation, have drawn outsized scrutiny, triggering protests, heightened security and internal reviews. A Special Investigation Team (SIT) formed by the Nashik police is probing eight accused persons  across nine FIRs, which include charges of mental and sexual harassment, obscene remarks, unwanted physical contact, hostile workplace conditions and hurting religious sentiments. For a company with more than 584,000 employees globally, the Nashik business process outsourcing (BPO) unit, spread across 5,000 sq ft and employing around 170 people across two shifts for a large banking client, would typically be a rounding-off error. Yet, on the ground, the situation appeared tense on April 16. Sources told Moneycontrol that employees were offered work-from-home arrangements amid safety concerns, particularly after an alleged attempt by a local political group to vandalise the office earlier in the week. The office, otherwise, continues to remain operational. Related Stories TCS ties up with Anthropic, to roll out Claude AI access to 50,000 employees TCS will take strict action if any associate is found responsible: N Chandrasekaran on Nashik case TCS AI revenue nears $2.5 billion annualised; AI agents could match employee count: N Chandrasekaran When this reporter reached the gates of Ashoka Business Enclave on that day, inside which the TCS office is located, at least a dozen police officers were guarding the premises. Inside the compound, private security personnel screened every passerby. Meanwhile, a protest led by Devyani Pharande, a BJP MLA from Nashik (Central), saw a large gathering of women raising slogans outside the complex. In response to the developments, TCS has hired Deloitte and Trilegal as independent advisors to support its internal investigation, which is being overseen by Aarthi Subramanian, President and Chief Operating Officer of TCS. The company has also constituted an Oversight Committee, chaired by independent director Keki Mistry, to review findings and recommend further action. Protests outside TCS Nashik office on April 16 | Photo courtesy: Debangana Ghosh FIR trail The case originated with a complaint filed on March 25 by a 23-year-old woman at the Deolali Camp police station in Nashik. The FIR named Danish Sheikh, Tausif Attar and Nida Khan, alleging rape, sexual harassment and religious coercion between 2022 and 2026. Subsequently, eight more FIRs were registered at the Mumbai Naka police station by early April. Moneycontrol has reviewed copies of all nine FIRs. Across these complaints, the key accused include Danish Sheikh (named in two FIRs), Tausif Attar (six FIRs), Shafi Shaikh (four FIRs), Raza Memon (five FIRs), Shahrukh Qureshi (four FIRs), Asif Ansari (two FIRs), Nida Khan (one FIR) and Ashwini Chainani (one FIR). Nida Khan, a telecaller and process associate based in Pune, faces allegations related to hurting religious sentiments. Ashwini Chainani, a delivery operations head also based in Pune and responsible for the Nashik unit, has been accused of inaction on one of the complaints. Neither holds an HR role at the Nashik facility. The first major FIR Danish had known the 23-year-old complainant since January 2022. He was a college senior, and they would often meet as friends, the complainant said in the FIR. Later, the accused tried to get physically intimate with her in mid of 2022, on the pretext of marriage, and the complainant resisted his advances. After she graduated that year with a BCA degree, Danish suggested that she apply to TCS, where he worked. The complainant got the job as an associate in 2023 and started spending time with him and his colleagues, Tausif and Nida, at the office canteen. The four had a WhatsApp group, where Tausif and Nida would allegedly discuss the differences between Hinduism and Islam with the complainant and try to convince her that their religion was superior. According to the FIR, Danish, on occasions, had hurt the complainant’s religious sentiments. The allegations got murkier in August 2024, when Danish and the complainant were out on a bike ride, and he took her to a resort on Trimbak Road in Nashik and allegedly forced her to have sex with him, according to the FIR. Following this, Tausif allegedly began blackmailing her by threatening to disclose the relationship to her family if she resisted his advances, the FIR said. In February 2026, Danish’s wife reached out to the complainant, revealing that he is already married and has two children. When confronted, Danish said he never intended to marry the complainant. The FIR was then registered on March 25. Nida is currently absconding, and Nashik Police has sent three search teams to find her. Her lawyers, Rahul Kasliwal and Baba Sayyed, filed an anticipatory bail plea at Nashik Sessions Court on Saturday. Nashik police team positioned outside Ashoka Business Enclave| Photo courtesy: Debangana Ghosh What did the other FIRs say? The complainant informed the police of similar mental and sexual harassment happening with other colleagues in the office. The police acted swiftly, forming an SIT led by Assistant Police Commissioner (Crime) Sandeep Mitke of Nashik Police. “After the first complaint, we sent a group of female police officers to get in touch with other women employees in the office to encourage them to come forward and file complaints. That led to nine FIRs getting registered,” Mitke told Moneycontrol. “The other FIRs were focused on molestation allegations, passing uncomfortable comments on the women’s attire and personal lives. FIRs also claimed the accused would talk down on other religions, mainly Hinduism, while promoting Islamic practices,” he added. Among the nine FIRs, eight were filed by women and one by a male employee. Most of the women targeted were aged around 23-25 years and came from humble, economically weaker backgrounds, the police said. Two complainants are in their mid-30s. According to the FIRs accessed, a common pattern of alleged harassment involved repeatedly asking women intrusive questions about their private lives, despite them not wanting to answer. This included questions on whether they had physical relationships with their boyfriends, whether they were going on a honeymoon with their spouse, commenting on a woman with Polycystic Ovarian Disease (PCOD), and telling one of the complainants to work on her body and lose weight. A complainant stated, “In December 2024, Shafi Shaikh sat next to me on a chair for no reason and on the pretext of helping me in my work, he deliberately tried to rub my feet with his feet. He also touched my chest with his hands on the pretext of using the keyboard. I understood his intention, and when I immediately moved my chair aside, he smiled at me and left from there.” “One Sunday in February 2026, during lunchtime, Tausif, who worked in the dialer team in our office, came to me and asked about my health. I told him that I recently had a miscarriage and was not feeling well. At that time, Tausif said, 'I will give you the mobile number of a cleric in Ajmer. I assure you that after going to Ajmer, you will have a child. You should go there once.' I found it embarrassing for him to talk about my personal life in this way, so I did not respond at the time. But he has repeatedly tried to discuss this subject even when I did not want to,” said another complainant in the FIR. A complainant, who got married in 2025, said in her FIR mentioning Shahrukh, “On March 19, I had come to the office wearing a saree for Gudi Padwa. At that time, around 12.00 pm, while I was in the office, Shahrukh came and sat next to me, looked at me strangely and said, "Today you look different, let’s go out somewhere today?" I felt uncomfortable listening to his words. I got up and left from there.” She had verbally complained about Shahrukh and her team leader, Raza Memon, who would ask uncomfortable personal questions at the workplace, to operations head Ashwini Chainani in February 2026 and March 2026. The complainant alleged that Ashwini didn’t take any action and told her, “Why do you want to get highlighted? Let it go.” Public prosecutor Kiran Bendbhar, who is representing eight out of the nine complainants, told Moneycontrol that Ashwini Chainani was also in fact a part of the company’s Prevention of Sexual Harassment (POSH) committee, yet she failed to raise the complaints. TCS has a common POSH committee for its Pune and Nashik offices, sources said. Bendbhar said that Shafi Shaikh is an “accused in several cases”. Back in 2022, a complainant sent an email to senior manager Nitin Kapoor, alleging that Shafi had stared at her inappropriately, and he was then shifted to another department. “Women have been facing these issues from 2022 to 2026. To continue in their jobs, they were avoiding filing complaints. Many of them are from poorer families. But now that these cases are in the court, we expect more victims to come forward and file formal complaints,” Bendbhar said. ‘No Forceful Conversion’ Advocate Kasliwal, who is also representing Raza, Tausif, and Asif, said that so far the remand reports by police do not indicate any forceful conversion. “TCS is a reputed company and hires people after thorough verifications. These victims and the accused are all well-educated and qualified. If this is a caste or religion issue, then I don’t think the victim would have taken three years to lodge a complaint. One of the accused had an affair with a girl. But just because they are from different religions, we can’t say the victim was forced to convert,” Kasliwal told Moneycontrol. He added, “This is a case of an office affair gone wrong, but it is aggravating the situation merely because they are from different castes. If it’s a voluntary conversion, it’s still their right. There are no allegations of forceful conversion anywhere. There may have been instances of accused opposing Hindu religion, but that doesn’t mean they have asked the other person to accept Muslim religion.” Kasliwal argued that all the allegations relate to individual conduct rather than the company, adding that whether the behaviour is appropriate depends on the relationship between the parties and the intent behind the act. He also said the offences carry sentences of under seven years, and according to the Supreme Court, such cases do not necessarily warrant immediate arrest. Why didn’t the senior managers act? SIT’s Mitke said the victims were scared because none of the managers they had been verbally raising complaints with took any action. The identities of some of these managers were revealed in the FIRs. In one of the FIRs, Raza himself was the senior with whom a complainant had shared her concern. Mitke, however, noted that senior manager Kapoor did write several emails to Chainani over the past couple of years complaining against the group of accused, but those were work-related emails. “Nitin Kapoor did write to Chainani regarding work disciplinary issues of the accused gang. They would come to the office late, and their work too wasn’t up to the mark. They would even bully colleagues. But Chainani didn’t take any action,” Mitke said. Around 78 emails were sent to Chainani, which are currently under police probe. Responding to Moneycontrol’s detailed queries, a TCS spokesperson said, “We wish to reiterate the statement issued concerning the matter on April 17, 2025. We are constrained on offering comments on specific queries as these are matters of present investigation.” “We will also request you to exercise caution and diligence in reporting on these matters rather than depending on hearsay as advised by the investigating authorities in their social media post,” the spokesperson added. Who is Ashwini Chainani? Chainani has been a TCS employee for over 20 years, having led many projects and teams. She is based out of the IT firm’s Pune office and would visit the Nashik office once a month or as needed. Advocate Mayur Deshpande, who, along with BR Deshpande, is representing Chainani, denied all the allegations against her. Deshpande told Moneycontrol , that she never got a complaint from the subordinate in question and the 78 emails under investigation, citing issues are all “work related” and not directly linked to the sexual harassment complaints. “My client lives in Pune, and she is not even the HR person. She was in the middle of handing over and transitioning to a different project and had initiated the process in September 2025, following some disagreements with an immediate senior and junior employee on her team. March 2026 was supposed to be her last month,” Deshpande said. He added, “She was called for questioning by the SIT between March 29 and 31. She travelled to the Nashik office, recorded her statement and came back. Suddenly, her name came up in a FIR filed on April 2, and she was taken into police custody. The complainant could have mentioned this while the SIT was speaking to their employees during their visit in March end. It’s shocking that she suddenly lodged a complaint.” While other accused continue to be in police custody, Chainani has been moved to judicial custody till April 28. Additionally, the SIT team has reserved the right to keep Chainani in police custody for 10 days till May 19 as required for the investigation. Hurting religious sentiments Amid this, a nationwide uproar has cast the complaints as involving forced religious conversion, though eight of the FIRs only cited instances of hurting religious sentiments, with only one exception involving a 35-year-old male employee. The 35-year-old, who claims to be a rudraksh-wearing follower of Hindu saint Ramdas Swami, alleged that he was forced to eat non-vegetarian food by Danish and Tausif. In 2023, his team leader, Tausif, had allegedly taken him to his house during Ramzan Eid and made him wear his religious cap and recite namaz. He took a picture of the complainant and posted it in a WhatsApp group, where he was later added. The employee alleged in the FIR that the accused insulted Hindu gods and in one instance called Chhatrapati Sambhaji Maharaj a “slave of Muslim religion.” “Tausif and Danish told me you tried taking medicine, yet your wife did not have a child, so send her to me. At that time, we had an argument, and Tausif had hit me in anger and threatened to kill me. After this, I had informed our Ashwini Madam,” the employee said. He added, “When Tausif and Danish were telling me about their religion, I started ignoring them, and they tried to harass me through various ways. I understood that both of them were trying to convert me by speaking against Hinduism in our office, so I was against it. That is why they were very angry with me.” Nashik Police Commissioner Sandeep Karnik said that his team has written to the National Investigation Agency (NIA), the Anti-Terrorism Squad (ATS) and the State Intelligence Department (SID) as well to examine whether any extremist organisations were linked to the accused in these cases. Identity of Nashik unit HR head Unclear Amid all of the chaos, the identity of the HR manager for the Nashik unit remains unclear. While most of the complaints at the company level were conveyed verbally, an email to Kapoor in 2022 led to Shafi being shifted to a different department, a move that would typically involve HR oversight. On April 17, TCS confirmed through a statement that no formal complaints were raised through its POSH channels in the last four years. “While detailed reviews are still underway, a preliminary review of the systems and records pertaining to the Nashik unit indicates that we have not received any complaints of the nature that are being alleged on either our ethics or POSH channels,” TCS CEO and MD K Krithivasan had said. The IT services behemoth reiterated that it holds itself to the “highest standards of employee welfare and institutional conduct.”

Title: TCS Nashik case: Police sends three teams to search for accused Nida Khan
Article: Nida Khan Nashik police searching for Nida Khan in TCS harassment case Khan was a process associate, not an HR manager at TCS Several accused are in custody; SIT is investigating the case Did our AI summary help? Nashik police on April 17 said that they have dispatched three teams to search for Nida Khan, one of the accused in the Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) Nashik sexual harassment case, citing that she is absconding. Khan's name came up in one of the nine FIRs lodged for allegedly assisting the complainant to learn to observe Roza, wearing a hijab. The main allegation is of hurting religious sentiments. Her lawyer Baba Sayyed told Moneycontrol that he will be filing an anticipatory bail petition at the Nashik sessions court over the next two-three days. Nashik Police said Khan's husband was detained and interrogated within the limits of Mumbra Police Station in Mumbai. During the investigation, he informed that the accused Nida had gone to her relatives. Accordingly, when the police team went to the house of the concerned relatives for search, the house was found locked. The mobile phones of Khan and her relatives were switched off and the search for the accused is currently underway. Related Stories TCS ties up with Anthropic, to roll out Claude AI access to 50,000 employees TCS will take strict action if any associate is found responsible: N Chandrasekaran on Nashik case TCS AI revenue nears $2.5 billion annualised; AI agents could match employee count: N Chandrasekaran Meanwhile, TCS CEO and MD K Krithivasan clarified that Khan, whose name has appeared repeatedly in press reports with the designation of HR manager, held no such title or authority within the organisation. She worked as a process associate without any leadership responsibilities and was not involved in recruitment functions. What has happened so far The case first came into light when in March, when a woman working at TCS’ Nashik BPO unit filed a complaint alleging that a colleague had maintained a physical relationship with her by promising marriage. During the investigation, police registered eight more FIRs related to mental and sexual harassment faced between 2022 and 2026, along with inaction by the senior managers. There were also allegations of hurting religious sentiments by a few complainants. The action was taken based on intelligence inputs received by the Nashik Police Commissioner’s office. As of now, accused Raza Memon, Shafi Shaikh, Asif Ansari, Shah Rukh Qureshi, Tausif Attar, and Danish Sheikh are in police custody. Another accused Ashwini Chainani is in judicial custody for her alleged inaction when the employees raised sexual harassment complaints against the other accused. The case is currently under investigation by a special investigation team (SIT) led by assistant commissioner of police (Crime) Sandeep Mitke.

Title: TCS Nashik case: No POSH complaints from employees who filed FIRs
Article: Representative image TCS says no formal POSH complaints received in Nashik cases Independent panel set up; Deloitte and Trilegal onboarded Police have arrested six accused; investigation ongoing Did our AI summary help? Tata Consultancy Services’ (TCS) has not received any formal complaints through its Prevention of Sexual Harassment (POSH) channels from the complainants in the Nashik branch sexual harassment cases that between 2022 to 2026 In a statement on April 17, TCS CEO and MD K Krithivasan said, “While detailed reviews are still underway, a preliminary review of the systems and records pertaining to the Nashik unit indicates that we have not received any complaints of the nature that are being alleged on either our ethics or POSH channels.” The IT services behemoth reiterated that it holds itself to the “highest standards of employee welfare and institutional conduct.” “As we have stated before, we have a zero-tolerance policy towards any form of coercion or misconduct. We are focused on supporting our employees and ensuring a safe and respectful workplace across all our locations. We continue to extend full cooperation to the law enforcement agencies so that the matter is investigated thoroughly, transparently and brought to a rightful conclusion,” Krithivasan said. TCS sets up independent panel Related Stories TCS ties up with Anthropic, to roll out Claude AI access to 50,000 employees TCS will take strict action if any associate is found responsible: N Chandrasekaran on Nashik case TCS AI revenue nears $2.5 billion annualised; AI agents could match employee count: N Chandrasekaran The company onboarded Deloitte and law firm Trilegal as independent counsel to support its internal inquiry in the Nashik case, which is being led by Aarthi Subramanian, President and COO of TCS. A dedicated Oversight Committee, chaired by independent director Keki Mistry, has been formed to receive the investigation's findings and act on any resulting recommendations. The findings of internal investigation will be presented to the Oversight Committee. What happened so far The case first came into light when in March, when a woman working at TCS’ Nashik BPO unit filed a complaint alleging that a colleague had maintained a physical relationship with her by promising marriage. During the investigation, police registered eight more FIRs related to mental and sexual harassment faced between 2022 and 2026, along with inaction by the senior managers. There were also allegations of attempting religious conversion by a few complainants. The action was taken based on intelligence inputs received by the Nashik Police Commissioner’s office. As of now, accused Raza Memon, Shafi Shaikh, Asif Ansari, Shah Rukh Qureshi, Tausif Attar, and Danish Sheikh are in police custody. A seventh accused Nida Khan is currently absconding, and Nashik police is searching for her. Another accused Ashwini Chainani is in judicial custody for her alleged inaction when the employees raised sexual harassment complaints against the other accused. The case is currently under investigation by a special investigation team (SIT) led by assistant commissioner of police (Crime) Sandeep Mitke.

Title: ‘Made to work alone on rooftop’: TCS Nashik woman employee makes explosive ‘soft targets’ claim
Article: TCS Nashik employee details harassment claims TCS Nashik staffer claims isolation, belongings seized Young female staff reportedly targeted and exploited at workplace Police probe ongoing; several staff, including HR, suspended Did our AI summary help? Fresh allegations have emerged from the ongoing controversy at the Nashik office of Tata Consultancy Services, with a woman employee claiming she was forced to work in isolation on a rooftop and had her personal belongings confiscated. Speaking to NDTV, the employee, who has been with the company for six years, said she was transferred to the Nashik office and then made to sit alone on a terrace, away from the main workplace. She alleged that her phone, bag, and other personal items were routinely taken away under the pretext of security. "I was made to work alone on the rooftop, and my phone and bag were confiscated, Whenever I came downstairs to use the washroom or for other purposes, my mobile phone, bag, and all personal belongings were confiscated under the pretext of security or other excuses." "We have seen that many of the young employees who work in the company were being exploited. They were being brainwashed," she added, as quoted by NDTV. As per reports, the woman further alleged that young female employees were being specifically targeted and exploited at the workplace. According to her, women aged between 20 and 25 were seen as “soft targets” and subjected to manipulation and pressure. She also claimed that even the human resources department failed to address complaints effectively. Related Stories TCS ties up with Anthropic, to roll out Claude AI access to 50,000 employees No further downsizing planned; still need to attract the best talent: TCS Chairman N Chandrasekaran Far from being mortal threat, AI is the most significant opportunity yet for TCS: Chairman N Chandra... "They thought that it would be easy to trap them. If you have any complaints, then you go to your HR. But here, even the HR was scared," she added. "Thank God I survived otherwise, the same thing would have happened to me today,” she said as per NDTV. Her statements come amid a wider investigation into serious allegations at the Nashik unit, including sexual harassment, coercion, and misconduct. Police are currently probing multiple complaints filed by women employees, and several staff members, including an HR manager, have already been arrested and suspended. According to a report by NDTV, another employee reportedly alleged that workers were pressured into personal relationships and even influenced on religious lines, adding to the gravity of the case. Authorities have formed a Special Investigation Team (SIT) to examine the matter in detail. The revelations have sparked widespread concern about workplace safety and corporate accountability. The employee said she felt fortunate to have escaped serious harm and urged other women to speak up against what she described as a “dark culture” within the office. As investigations continue, the case has raised serious questions about employee protection mechanisms and oversight within large corporate setups. All arrested staff have been suspended by TCS.

Title: TCS Nashik sexual harassment case a failure of internal systems, processes: Industry experts
Article: Representative image TCS faces criticism for delay in handling harassment complaints Investigation led by TCS COO Aarthi Subramanian is ongoing Experts call it a failure in implementing the POSH policy Did our AI summary help? Tata Consultancy Services’ delay in responding to the sexual harassment and religious conversion complaints at its Nashik facility is a systemic failure in implementing the Prevention of Sexual Harassment (POSH) policy, say industry experts. Tata Sons Chairman N Chandrasekaran said on April 13 that a “thorough investigation is underway to establish the facts and identify all individuals responsible for this situation”. The company has appointed TCS chief operating officer (COO) Aarthi Subramanian to lead the investigation. Apart from the seven arrested accused, HR manager Nida Khan, who worked at the company’s Pune office and who allegedly ignored the victims' complaints, is absconding, reports have said. Reacting to the oversight at the senior level in the company, TV Mohandas Pai, former chief financial officer and HR head of Infosys and chairman of Aarin Capital Partners, said, “When a man grabs a woman in public and tries to assault her, people around should protest and support the woman. But clearly, everyone is living in an atmosphere of fear. There is a failure of systems and processes — absolutely.” Related Stories TCS ties up with Anthropic, to roll out Claude AI access to 50,000 employees TCS will take strict action if any associate is found responsible: N Chandrasekaran on Nashik case TCS AI revenue nears $2.5 billion annualised; AI agents could match employee count: N Chandrasekaran “There appears to be pandering to religious fanatics. There is open, aggressive behaviour from such groups. This has happened in other companies too — I’ve personally seen instances where people gang up together.” According to Pai, better communication channels are needed for employees to directly connect with managers two-levels above them. Employees should have regular “skip meetings” with a manager two levels above, without their immediate manager present. “This doesn’t seem to be happening, because the issue has persisted for a long time,” Pai said. FIRs and arrests so far The arrested individuals have been identified as Asif Ansari, Shafi Sheikh, Shah Rukh Qureshi, Raza Memon, Tausif Attar, Danish Sheikh and Ashwini Chainani. The case first came into light when in March, when  a woman working at TCS filed a complaint alleging that a colleague had maintained a physical relationship with her by promising marriage. During the investigation, police registered eight more FIRs (first information reports) related to sexual harassment and religious conversion cases, as per a media report. The action was taken based on intelligence inputs received by the Nashik Police Commissioner’s office. Nashik police had deployed some of its constables to go undercover to gather information, and work as TCS employees between February and March. The case is currently under investigation by a special investigation team (SIT) led by assistant commissioner of police (Crime) Sandeep Mitke. Failure of implementing POSH Act With more than 584,519 staff globally, TCS is one of the largest private sector employers in India, with well-structured employee policies in place being a Tata Group company. “TCS has well established processes for raising complaints. Probably the employees did not follow them either due to ignorance or due to possible ramifications that they thought that they would undergo if they are complaining,” said Aditya Mishra, MD & CEO, CIEL HR, a recruitment and staffing firm. “In this particular case, it was all people with position of power and authority.” Several accused who have been arrested are in team-leader positions. Mishra said companies have whistleblower policies and there are three levels of escalations involved. Members from both genders need to be a part of the Internal Complaint Committee (ICC) and sexual harassment committees. Employees are supposed to be trained annually for them to understand what would amount to sexual harassment. Pai called the case a complete “failure” in implementing the POSH policy. Any complaint must go to a high-level, independent channel such as a dedicated webpage not to a direct manager, he said. “Managers are often at a lower level and complaints must instead go to a body supervised by the audit committee, not management,” he said. In the TCS case, Nashik police sent six of its women officers undercover to work at the facility between March and April to gather information. Is it a Tier-II, III city problem? “There seems to be a pattern emerging at TCS in HR practices, first with the bribe-for-jobs case, then the layoffs and now this Nashik case. TCS took action and accountability only after media attention and social media pressure,” said Pareekh Jain, CEO, EIIRTrend. “This is like some bureaucracy and the complaints may have not reached the HR head. People at CXO levels should have been aware of this and the head of HR must be questioned.” He wondered if this would have happened at one of TCS’ Bengaluru, Mumbai or Chennai offices. “TCS is spreading to tier-II and tier-III cities towns, but employee wellbeing policies were clearly ineffective in this case. Policies need to be implemented in letter and spirit,” he said. What did the company say? Chandrasekaran called the allegations emerging from the Nashik unit “gravely concerning and anguishing” . “This incident is being treated with the utmost seriousness. Action has already been initiated against the accused employees, and the company is extending its full cooperation to the ongoing investigations. The Tata Group maintains a zero-tolerance policy towards any form of coercion or misconduct by its employees,” he said.

Title: A first for TCS: Revenue growth falls despite strong deals, margins
Article: In FY26, TCS reported revenue of Rs 2.67 lakh crore, up 4.6 percent year-on-year IT behemoth Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) has reported a 0.5 percent year-on-year decline in USD terms in FY26 revenue growth, the first time since it went public in 2004. This is coming in a year when the IT services giant also reported a strong order book and good operating margin despite the challenging macroeconomic scenario. The divergence suggests a structural mismatch i.e., strong deal momentum but weakening revenue conversion. Moreover, India's services leader’s 2.4 percent annual revenue decline in constant currency terms also has analysts worried. The slowdown has become more visible after the BSNL deal tapered off, with macro pressures and rising competition for large deals weighing on growth. Related Stories The Reading List: June 12, 2026 Business in the Week Ahead (June 15-19, 2026) UPI’s transaction volume and user adoption growth metrics see sharp slowdown “With the BSNL deal tapering off, TCS’s underlying slowdown has become more visible, even as competition for large deals has intensified and pricing has become more aggressive,” Pareekh Jain, CEO of Pareekh Consulting and EIIRTrend said. Rightly so, revenue from India de-grew by 28.6 percent YoY in CC, which was earlier a strong support when North America and Europe slowed down amid US trade tariff tensions and macro concerns. “The bigger issue is not just the quarter in isolation, but what it says about the broader demand environment for large IT services firms. While reported growth was supported by currency tailwinds, the underlying trend still reflects a sluggish spending cycle, especially in discretionary technology programs,” said Harshal Dasani, Business Head at INVasset PMS. What gives? Industry analysts believe it’s a structural shift in how revenue is getting generated from the same large deals, as customers demand faster outcomes with lower human efforts in a post AI world. The Mumbai-headquartered IT firm had also revamped its business strategy and operations to align with its AI ambitions in FY26, ending the year with $2.3 billion in annualised AI revenue, accounting for nearly 7.5 percent of the company’s overall revenue. According to Phil Fersht, founder and CEO of HFS Research, TCS’ annual revenue decline in CC terms, is the clearest evidence yet that the industry's “core commercial logic is being rewritten.” While it’s clear that the IT services demand is far from disappearing as deal pipelines remain resilient with large deals continuing to come in and margins holding above 25 percent, the way the demand is being consumed is fast changing, noted Fersht. In his blog post, Fersht said, “Clients are no longer buying effort. They are demanding outcomes. AI is compressing delivery timelines, reducing headcount intensity, and forcing providers to deliver more value for less revenue. That creates a brutal tension: strong bookings on one side, muted revenue realization on the other.” What did the management say? TCS reported $12 billion in Q4 order book, that includes three mega deals mostly renewals from major customers such as Marks & Spencer. For the full-year, the deal total contract value (TCV) stood at $40.7 billion with five multi-year mega deals in total. Meanwhile, for the full year FY26, TCS reported revenue of Rs 2.67 lakh crore, up 4.6 percent year-on-year, while operating margin improved to 25 percent and net margin to 19.8 percent -- both the highest levels in the last four years. This gives TCS enough fuel to hope for a consistent FY27 amid the geopolitical uncertainties. “Even in a year marked by global uncertainty, we have demonstrated resilience, discipline and the ability to lead through complexity. Our performance FY26 is not just sound execution, but enduring trust from clients who are committing to longer-term partnerships,” said TCS CEO and MD K Krithivasan. He added, “Our client relationships are expanding across revenue bands. Accelerating AI momentum and foundational investments like HyperVault, are building the next phase of TCS with conviction. As enterprises increasingly seek partners who can deliver certainty, accountability and outcomes end to end, TCS stands exceptionally well positioned.” After a year full of reworking business models, venturing in building data centers, trimming workforce globally, Krithivasan said that the company is entering the new financial year with “confidence, clarity of purpose, and an unwavering focus on delivering growth with resilience and trust.” How are brokerages reacting? Even as TCS reported a rare YoY contraction in constant currency terms, brokerages have largely refrained from calling it a structural inflection, instead attributing the slowdown to near-term factors and betting on a recovery led by deal wins and AI-led demand. Brokerage Elara, for instance, pinned the weakness on the tapering of the BSNL deal and maintained that underlying international growth remains intact, while HDFC Securities acknowledged the constant currency decline but framed it as transitory, pointing to improving deal wins, vertical recovery, and stronger visibility into FY27. Brokerage Nomura, however, strikes a slightly more nuanced note, flagging that while growth was broadly in line, underlying trends remain uneven with continued macro uncertainty weighing on client spending. However, it stops short of calling it a structural break. Instead, it highlights strong deal wins and a pick up in AI-led projects. The brokerage expects FY27 to be better on the back of strong pipeline and improved execution.

Title: Buy Tata Consultancy Services; target of Rs 3450: Prabhudas Lilladher
Article: buy TCS TCS Watchlist Portfolio Message Follow NSE/BSE Select NSE LIVE BSE LIVE Trade Day High Day Low Volume (NSE) More × Add to Watchlist × Prabhudas Lilladher's research report on Tata Consultancy Services TCS Q4’s performance of 1.2% QoQ CC growth was largely in line with our estimates of 1.3% growth, aided by broad-based growth across all major geographies and segments except BFSI & CMT. AI revenue momentum continued with annualized revenue of USD 2.3 bn, up 27.8% QoQ and now forms 7.7% of revenue mix. However, geopolitical conflicts are not only having a direct impact on Travel and Transportation verticals, but delaying decisions within key verticals, especially BFSI. The vertical (BFSI) reported 2.9% USD growth in FY26, which will likely create a headwind in FY27E. The deal TCV growth remains weak (3.6% YoY) at 40.7b, despite the company winning 5 mega deals in FY26. Considering the factors above, we are trimming our revenue estimates by 40/30bps; expect CC revenue growth of 4.5% including inorganic (30bps) in FY27E followed by 5.1% in FY28E, respectively. On the margin front, we expect improvement of 30bps & 20bps (earlier 50bps & 30bps), respectively in FY27E & FY28E on account of forging partnerships with frontier model providers and investments in talent re-skilling activities. Outlook However, the management indicated that achieving 26% EBIT margin continues to be the long-term aspiration. We assign 20x to FY28E EPS that translates a TP of 3,450. Maintain BUY. Title: No direct business impact of the Middle East conflict, says TCS CEO K Krithivasan
Article: TCS CEO K Krithivasan TCS faces no direct impact from West Asia conflict so far All TCS employees in Middle East are safe and supported TCS Q4 FY26 net profit jumps 29 percent to Rs 13,718 crore Did our AI summary help? Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) CEO and MD K Krithivasan said that the IT services behemoth isn’t facing any direct impact of the ongoing West Asia war. The company’s management assured that the employees in the region remain safe too. Speaking at the company’s Q4FY26 earnings conference on April 9, Krithivasan said, “Any direct impact of the geopolitical situation so far has been restricted to the Middle East. Apart from travel and transportation industry, we have not seen major impact in other industries so far.” “If things continue or if this results in further supply chain disruption or any other secondary issues, it may have an impact. At this time, we have not been hearing any other specific concerns from our clients and other industries or other geographies,” he added. CHRO Sudeep Kunnumal thanked the company’s employees and their families stationed in West Asia for their “utmost dedication and resilience during these tough times.” “All our employees and their families in the Middle East are safe and we are in constant touch with them, providing the necessary support,” he said. Related Stories TCS ties up with Anthropic, to roll out Claude AI access to 50,000 employees TCS will take strict action if any associate is found responsible: N Chandrasekaran on Nashik case TCS AI revenue nears $2.5 billion annualised; AI agents could match employee count: N Chandrasekaran TCS reported a robust set of numbers for fiscal fourth quarter results . TCS posted a Q4 FY26 consolidated net profit of Rs 13,718 crore, a jump of nearly 29 percent from the previous quarter, broadly in line with a CNBC TV18 poll estimate of Rs 13,727 crore. Revenue from operations rose 5.4 percent quarter-on-quarter to Rs 70,698 crore, ahead of the poll estimate of 3.4 percent growth. Operating margin for Q4 stood at 25.3 percent, up 10 bps sequentially. For the full year FY26, TCS reported revenue of Rs 2.67 lakh crore, up 4.6 percent year-on-year, while operating margin improved to 25 percent and net margin to 19.8 percent -- both the highest levels in the last four years. The total contract value (TCV) for Q4 stood at $12 billion, while full year order book was about $40.7 billion. TCS reported five mega deal wins in FY26.

Title: TCS wage bill rises Rs 10,000 crore despite 23,000 fewer employees in FY26; restructuring at Rs 1,388 cr
Article: TCS TCS incurred Rs 1,388 crore in restructuring expenses in FY26 Over 23,000 employees exited, headcount now at 584,519 Focus on AI skills and future-ready workforce continues into FY27 Did our AI summary help? IT behemoth Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) saw its employee costs rise by around Rs 10,000 crore in FY26, even as it reduced its workforce by over 23,000 employees, with the IT major also incurring Rs 1,388 crore in restructuring expenses during the year. The company said Rs 1,268 crore of the total restructuring provision was utilised in FY26 itself, indicating that most of the cost tied to employee exits and related measures has already been absorbed. The restructuring exercise traces back to July 2025, when the Tata Group firm confirmed Moneycontrol ’s exclusive story to realign its workforce. Moreover, the Mumbai-headquartered firm reported a net reduction of over 23,000 employees , taking its total headcount to 584,519 at the end of the year. Additionally, TCS’s employee expenses rose to around Rs 1.5 lakh crore in FY26, reflecting an increase of roughly Rs 10,000 crore over the previous year. Related Stories TCS ties up with Anthropic, to roll out Claude AI access to 50,000 employees Microsoft Xbox leads fresh layoff wave as big tech doubles down on AI TCS will take strict action if any associate is found responsible: N Chandrasekaran on Nashik case “In Q4, we continued to invest in a future‑ready workforce with strong additions across experienced talent and campus hires. Building an AI‑first culture and equipping our people with AI‑ready skills remained a key priority in FY26 and will continue into FY27, as we align closely with our customers’ evolving needs,” Sudeep Kunnumal, Chief HR Officer, was quoted as saying in the release. "The company is transitioning from a traditional IT services provider to a leader in AI-driven enterprise solutions, positioning itself as a full transformation partner rather than just an execution vendor. With ongoing investments in advanced AI technologies and infrastructure, TCS continues to add value for clients," said Biswajit Maity, Sr Principal Analyst, Gartner. As part of the move, the company released, or plans to release, certain associates whose deployment was not feasible, providing termination benefits in line with its internal policy. TCS classified these costs as “restructuring expenses” under exceptional items in its consolidated financial statements, given their size and nature. The sharp increase in restructuring spend also reflects a broader recalibration underway at India’s largest IT services exporter, as companies adjust workforce structures amid shifting demand and evolving skill requirements, particularly in areas such as AI and automation.

Title: TCS Q4 results: Annualised AI revenue grows to $2.3 billion as AI deployment gains pace
Article: Representative image TCS annualised AI revenue hits $2.3 billion in Q4 FY26 TCS announces strategic partnership with OpenAI for AI innovation Q4 net profit jumps 29 percent to Rs 13,718 crore Did our AI summary help? Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) on April 9 reported annualised AI revenue of $2.3 billion for the fourth quarter ending March 31 accounting for nearly 7.5 percent of the overall revenue. This was driven by strong momentum in deployment of AI solutions. This is the third time the IT services giant has shared its annualised AI revenue, which stood at $1.8 billion in the previous quarter. The company has worked on 5,500 AI projects till date. Aarthi Subramanian, Executive Director - President and Chief Operating Officer, TCS said, "FY26 marked a pivotal year for enterprise AI adoption. In Q4, our annualized AI revenues surpassed $2.3 billion, driven by the accelerated deployment of AI solutions.” “We experienced strong deal momentum across new services in enterprise transformation, digital engineering, and cloud modernization. Our investment in HyperVault was a catalyst in forging strategic partnerships with OpenAI, AMD and ABB, further strengthening our positioning across Infrastructure-to-Intelligence,” she added. Related Stories The FDI question at the heart of Zepto's IPO Trump rejects Iran's version of draft US deal, says drone attack on Indian ships 'totally unacceptab... Sensex, Nifty jump 2%: Market rally adds nearly Rs 10 lakh crore to investor wealth FY26 was significant in TCS’ history, as it revamped its company strategy to align with its AI ambitions. The IT firm has entered into data centre space announcing major investments through subsidiary HyperVault. In Q4, TCS announced a multi-dimensional strategic partnership with OpenAI, that spans multiple high-impact areas, including powering AI-led innovation across Tata Group companies, joint efforts to drive AI transformation across industries globally, setting up AI infrastructure and social impact. TCS’ HyperVault and OpenAI plans to develop AI infrastructure in India, with 100MW capacity in the initial phase, and an option to scale to 1 GW. This infrastructure will power next-generation AI workloads and position India as a global AI hub. Overall deal wins for the quarter came in at $12 billion, while full year order book rose to $40.7 billion. TCS reported a robust set of numbers in fiscal fourth quarter results on April 9. TCS posted a Q4 FY26 consolidated net profit of Rs 13,718 crore, a jump of nearly 29 percent from the previous quarter, broadly in line with a CNBC TV18 poll estimate of Rs 13,727 crore. Revenue from operations rose 5.4 percent quarter-on-quarter to Rs 70,698 crore, ahead of the poll estimate of 3.4 percent growth. Operating margin for Q4 stood at 25.3 percent. The company’s board has proposed a final dividend of Rs 31 per share for FY26, subject to shareholder approval at the annual general meeting. Total shareholder payout for the year stood at Rs 39,571 crore in the form of dividends.

Title: TCS Q4 order book grows 29% QoQ to $12 billion, full year deal wins at $40.7 billion
Article: Representative image TCS reported $12 billion in Q4 deal wins, beating estimates Annual AI revenue grew to $2.3 billion from $1.8 billion Q4 net profit jumped 29 percent to Rs 13,718 crore Did our AI summary help? India’s largest IT services company Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) on April 9 reported $12 billion in deal wins for the fourth quarter ending March 31, beating Street estimates despite macroeconomic uncertainties and Middle East tensions. For the full year FY26, TCS total contract value (TCV) stood at $40.7 billion. According to the company’s filings, the strong deal pipeline was driven by three mega deals won in Q4 and five overall mega deals in FY26. TCS also called out its annualised AI revenue of $2.3 billion, growing from $1.8 billion in the previous quarter. The TCV for the quarter was 1.64 per cent lower than the $12.2 billion reported in the year-ago period and was up 29 percent from the previous quarter. K Krithivasan, Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director of TCS, said “We are pleased to report the third consecutive quarter of sequential growth, supported by three mega deals and a $12 billion TCV, underscoring the strength of our five pillar strategy and our AI-led positioning across services." Related Stories TCS ties up with Anthropic, to roll out Claude AI access to 50,000 employees TCS will take strict action if any associate is found responsible: N Chandrasekaran on Nashik case TCS AI revenue nears $2.5 billion annualised; AI agents could match employee count: N Chandrasekaran “It is equally encouraging that this momentum was broad based across major markets and most industries. While the macro-economic headwinds continue, we see sustained customer conviction in technology investments, which positions us well for the opportunities ahead,” he added. TCS saw several deal renewals in Q4, including Rajarambapu Sahakari Bank and a five-year AI-led transformation deal extension from Swissport. New deal wins announced this quarter were a modernisation project with Janata Sahakari Bank, AI and factory modernisation for ABB, IT transformation for Finland-based Kalmar Oyj, multi-year AI deal with UK’s Pearson, among others. Among segments, growth was driven by energy, resources and utilities growing 7.3 percent YoY in CC, life sciences and healthcare grew 3.3 percent YoY in CC, followed by manufacturing that saw 3.1 percent YoY CC increase, technology and services grew 2.5 percent. Meanwhile, regional markets segment declined 12.8 percent YoY in CC and communications and media was down by 2.1 percent in YoY CC. BFSI, that accounts for over 30 percent of TCS' revenue grew by 0.4 percent YoY CC. Within geographies, growth was led by North America growing 2.5 percent in YoY CC and Continental Europe growing 1.0 percent YoY in CC. India was down by 23 percent in YoY CC. Middle East And Africa (MEA) grew 7.8 percent YoY in CC despite the ongoing war. TCS reported a robust set of numbers in fiscal fourth quarter results on April 9. TCS posted a Q4 FY26 consolidated net profit of Rs 13,718 crore, a jump of nearly 29 percent from the previous quarter, broadly in line with a CNBC TV18 poll estimate of Rs 13,727 crore. Revenue from operations rose 5.4 percent quarter-on-quarter to Rs 70,698 crore, ahead of the poll estimate of 3.4 percent growth. The company’s board has proposed a final dividend of Rs 31 per share for FY26, subject to shareholder approval at the annual general meeting. Total shareholder payout for the year stood at Rs 39,571 crore in the form of dividends.
