Title: TCS bags deal from travel tech firm Amadeus to accelerate modern airline retailing
Article: Representative image TCS partners with Amadeus to modernize airline retailing. TCS to create cloud-native SaaS for Amadeus Nevio platform. Partnership aims to enhance airline customer service operations. Did our AI summary help? Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) on March 19 announced that it has entered into a strategic partnership with travel technology company Amadeus to accelerate digital transformation in the travel and aviation sector, with a focus on modernising airline retailing and customer servicing. As part of the collaboration, TCS will develop the Service Center User Interface for Amadeus’ next-generation airline platform, Nevio. The interface will be built as a cloud-native Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) solution and integrated into the Nevio ecosystem, aimed at enhancing airline customer service operations. The partnership will see TCS play a key role in supporting the rollout of the Nevio platform to airlines globally, including implementation, integration, and ongoing delivery support. The companies said the joint effort will combine TCS’ capabilities in artificial intelligence (AI), cloud, data analytics, and platform engineering with Amadeus’ travel technology platforms. The Nevio Service Center is designed to enable airlines to manage offer and order lifecycles more efficiently, while leveraging AI-driven insights to deliver real-time, personalised retailing experiences. Cyril Tetaz, EVP Airline Solutions, Amadeus, said, “TCS is one of the world’s largest technology service providers, bringing expertise in travel as part of the Tata Group, which operates several airlines…By combining Amadeus’ trusted travel platform with AI-enabled capabilities, we are fostering cross-industry collaboration among suppliers, partners, and customers, we’re excited about this partnership and look forward to working together to improve the travel experience.” Related Stories TCS ties up with Anthropic, to roll out Claude AI access to 50,000 employees TCS will take strict action if any associate is found responsible: N Chandrasekaran on Nashik case TCS AI revenue nears $2.5 billion annualised; AI agents could match employee count: N Chandrasekaran Arun Pradeep Surendra Mohan, Business Head, Travel, Transportation & Hospitality, EMEA & APAC, TCS, added, “Our global strategic partnership with Amadeus marks an important step in reshaping the future of travel technology… Together, we are building scalable, intelligent solutions like the Amadeus Nevio Service Center that enable airlines to accelerate retail transformation, enhance operational agility, and deliver seamless, personalized experiences to travellers around the world.” Beyond airline retailing, the partnership is expected to expand into other areas such as airport operations and broader travel ecosystem solutions over time.

Title: Wipro shares fall over 11% in 2 days; should you buy, sell or hold?
Article: Wipro shares see profit booking in trade. Wipro shares fell over 11 percent after buyback record date. Analysts say decline is a price adjustment, not fundamentals. Stock nears key support zone; further fall if below Rs 176. Did our AI summary help? W W Watchlist Portfolio Message Follow NSE/BSE Select NSE LIVE BSE LIVE Trade Day High Day Low Volume (NSE) More × Add to Watchlist × Wipro shares declined more than 11 percent over the last two trading sessions, with the stock coming under pressure after the record date for the company's Rs 15,000-crore share buyback programme. The stock had fixed June 5 as the record date for the buyback. Wipro shares closed 2.91 percent lower on Friday and extended losses on Monday, ending 8.45 percent down at Rs 181.60 per share on the NSE. According to market analysts, the sharp fall reflects a price adjustment following the buyback record date rather than any deterioration in the company's underlying business fundamentals. Nishchal Jain, Quant Researcher at Share.Market by PhonePe, said the decline was largely driven by short-term investors exiting their positions after becoming eligible for the company's Rs 250 per share buyback offer. "The recent 11 percent decline in Wipro over two consecutive trading sessions represents a structural price adjustment rather than a core fundamental breakdown. The stock shed its corporate action premium after the June 5 record date and moved from around Rs 204 to the Rs 183-184 zone," he said. Related Stories IFCI shares hit 20% upper circuit as NSE likely to file DRHP next week Bank Nifty jumps 3%, all 14 stocks gain sharply; HDFC Bank, Axis Bank among top drivers of banking r... Nestle India declines 3% after FSSAI issues notice over alleged insect detection in Maggi; FMCG firm... Jain said the stock has now entered an important support area between Rs 180 and Rs 184, which has historically attracted institutional buying interest. He added that any recovery could face resistance around Rs 195 initially, followed by a stronger hurdle in the Rs 200-205 range. He further said broader factors such as movements in global bond yields and profit-booking across the Nifty IT index may have contributed to recent volatility. However, he noted that developments such as Wipro's expanded enterprise AI partnership with ServiceNow and its acquisition of an 80 per cent stake in Aggne Global support the company's longer-term growth outlook. For investors, Jain said the recent correction has reduced risk levels for long-term positions, provided the stock sustains above the Rs 180 mark on a weekly closing basis. Virat Jagad, Senior Technical Research Analyst at Bonanza, said Wipro is currently trading near a key long-term support zone of Rs 176-180, which has historically acted as a strong demand area. "The stock remains below its 20- and 50-month exponential moving averages, indicating a weak medium-term trend, while RSI is hovering near the lower range, reflecting subdued momentum," he said. Jagad cautioned that a decisive break below Rs 176 could trigger fresh selling pressure and drag the stock towards Rs 160 and Rs 145 levels. On the upside, he said any rebound may face resistance in the Rs 205-210 range, followed by Rs 230. He advised traders to closely track the Rs 176 support level, adding that a breach could lead to further downside pressure in the months ahead. Overall, analysts said the stock is approaching a critical support zone, with its ability to hold above key levels likely to determine the near-term trend. Title: Ex-Wipro employee alleges attempt at religious conversion by female colleague in Pune
Article: (File photo) A former female employee at Wipro's Pune office has alleged that her colleague harassed her over religious grounds and tried to force her into religious conversion. She claimed that due to this, she had to quit her job. She has filed a complaint with the Hinjewadi Police and served a legal notice to Wipro Technologies, alleging religious harassment, workplace discrimination and forced resignation, ANI reported. #WATCH | Pune, Maharashtra: A former employee has filed a complaint with the Hinjawadi Police and served a legal notice to Wipro Technologies, alleging religious harassment, workplace discrimination, and forced resignation. The victim and the complainant says, "These individuals… pic.twitter.com/38sChBywcy — ANI (@ANI) June 4, 2026 The complainant told ANI, "These individuals trap and coerce Hindu women, forcing them to choose between complying with their demands or resigning from their jobs... I had to bring to light the sheer amount of harassment and torture I endured over the past ten months... when we report these incidents to the company, they dismiss them and the matter is subsequently swept under the rug... Shahina Rafiq began harassing me from my very first day on the job... she tried to convince me to establish a physical relationship with Country Head Ramkumar and use that connection to secure a transfer to Dubai, where she could arrange my marriage with one of the Sheikhs, ensuring financial growth and sexual satisfaction..." Related Stories Pune IT professional loses Rs 4.43 crore to fake stock trading network on WhatsApp Wipro shares fall over 11% in 2 days; should you buy, sell or hold? Pune may soon get water supply once every 2 days; here's why PMC is concerned The woman alleged that her termination was "illegal" and wants her job back. She added, "I submitted a complaint yesterday. I have been summoned today regarding the FIR. They stated that they would conduct an investigation, following which an FIR would be formally registered... On April 24, 2026, we raised this issue once again. This occurred after my resignation, when I demanded that they provide me with a specific reason as to why I had been terminated... My demand is simple: I want my job back... My termination was illegal. I have reiterated this point time and again. Secondly, a dedicated institutional mechanism should be established within every private sector organisation..." The police are looking into every specific allegation raised by her as well as the company's internal records. “At Wipro, employee welfare, dignity, and respect are paramount. We maintain a zero-tolerance approach towards any form of misconduct, discrimination, harassment, or actions that compromise an individual's fundamental rights and freedoms," Wipro's official statement read. "We have been fully cooperating with the authorities in this matter and have provided all relevant documents and information with the Pune Police. As the matter is currently under investigation, we cannot comment on the specifics of the case. We remain committed to maintaining a safe, inclusive, and respectful workplace for all our employees," the statement added. This incident comes months after a similar case surfaced at TCS's Nashik office. Several survivors stepped forward with allegations of harassment and forced conversion against their colleagues. Many accused were arrested.

Title: Flawed AI, deepfakes and geopolitical conflicts pose major risks to business: Wipro
Article: . IT major Wipro has flagged the rapid adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI) as a major risk factor, warning that flawed algorithms, biases, and evolving regulations could pose significant legal, financial, and reputational threats to its business. In its FY26 annual report, the Bengaluru-headquartered company noted that while it is increasingly integrating generative and autonomous AI into its client offerings and internal operations, the technology remains "uncertain and evolving". "The development, adoption, and use of AI technologies remain uncertain and evolving, and we may not be able to successfully develop, deploy, or scale AI-enabled solutions or realise the anticipated benefits. Any inability to innovate or integrate these technologies effectively, or the failure of such technologies to perform as expected, could adversely affect our competitive position, operational efficiency, and financial results," Wipro stated. The company cautioned that AI systems operating with limited human intervention increase the risk of unintended outcomes. Deficiencies in these solutions, Wipro noted, could lead to project delays, failure to meet contractual service levels, disputes, and ultimately, the loss of business. The rise of AI automation, improved efficiency, and independent client tools could lower the demand for certain core services. Consequently, this might hurt prices, profit margins, service levels, and the mix of services offered by the firm. Related Stories Digitized Data, A.I. Agents, High Quality Assets- PNB MD & CEO Reveals The New Face of PSU Banks General Catalyst, India Quotient invest $1.5 million in kids-focussed AI startup Omli AI boom could unlock $280-billion opportunity across India’s data centre ecosystem by 2035: PwC Furthermore, the IT services provider highlighted that the use of AI increases its legal and regulatory exposure. "Clients may seek enhanced contractual protection relating to AI use, including representations, warranties, indemnities, audit rights and obligations concerning intellectual property ownership, data usage, cybersecurity, regulatory compliance, and AI-generated outputs. "If AI-enabled solutions cause harm to clients, their customers or other third parties, we could be subject to regulatory action, litigation, financial liability, reputational harm or increased compliance costs," Wipro said. Alongside operational AI risks, Wipro underscored the growing menace of highly sophisticated cyber threats. The company warned that the malicious use of advanced AI models to create deepfakes and AI-generated social engineering has significantly expanded the threat landscape, making the company and its third-party vendors vulnerable to data breaches and ransomware attacks. Beyond technological challenges, the company listed macroeconomic and geopolitical uncertainties as material risks to its growth. "Recent and continued geopolitical changes, including changes in tariffs and trade policies, where we or our clients operate, and other geopolitical events, conflicts or disruptions around the globe, may directly or indirectly hamper our growth," it said. Wipro, which derives approximately 62 per cent of its IT services revenue from the Americas and 27 per cent from Europe, noted that ongoing conflicts in the Middle East, South Asia, and between Russia and Ukraine could lead to supply-chain disruptions, inflationary pressures, and reduced business confidence. This, in turn, could prompt clients to delay, reduce, or reprioritise their discretionary technology spending. Additionally, the company warned that rising wage costs in India could diminish its historical competitive advantage against tech firms in the US and Europe, potentially squeezing profit margins.

Title: Wipro CEO Srinivas Pallia’s FY26 pay slips 8% to Rs 49.6 crore
Article: Wipro CEO Srinivas Pallia W W Watchlist Portfolio Message Follow NSE/BSE Select NSE LIVE BSE LIVE Trade Day High Day Low Volume (NSE) More × Add to Watchlist × Wipro Chief Executive Officer (CEO) and Managing Director Srinivas Pallia’s total compensation declined about 8 percent to nearly Rs 49.6 crore for fiscal year 2026, according to the company’s annual report. Pallia had earned about Rs 54 crore in FY25 , his first full year as CEO of the Bengaluru-headquartered IT services company. Pallia’s FY26 compensation of nearly Rs 49.6 crore was significantly lower than Infosys CEO Salil Parekh’s Rs 82.6 crore package , but higher than TCS CEO K Krithivasan’s FY26 remuneration of Rs 28 crore . According to Wipro ’s annual report filed with the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), Pallia earned total compensation of $5.29 million during FY26, equivalent to about Rs 49.6 crore based on the exchange rate used by the company. The compensation included salary and allowances, variable pay, stock-linked compensation and retirement benefits. Related Stories Ex-Wipro employee alleges attempt at religious conversion by female colleague in Pune Flawed AI, deepfakes and geopolitical conflicts pose major risks to business: Wipro The largest component of Pallia’s package came from stock-linked compensation and other benefits, which stood at about $2.48 million during the fiscal year. His salary and allowances were about $1.65 million, while commission and variable pay stood at about $1.05 million. Deferred long-term benefits were about $108,000. The compensation disclosure comes at a time when India’s IT services sector continues to navigate macroeconomic uncertainty and increasing investments in artificial intelligence-led transformation. Wipro chairman Rishad Premji earned total compensation of about $773,000 during FY26, according to the annual report.

Title: TCS, Infosys, Wipro took over 300,000 Microsoft Copilot licences in 6 months
Article: Representative image TCS, Infosys, and Wipro deploy Copilot to over 300,000 employees Infosys, TCS, and Wipro report over 85% active Copilot usage Copilot boosts productivity and saves workdays at Indian IT firms Did our AI summary help? Top Indian IT services players, including Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), Infosys and Wipro, have deployed Microsoft 365 Copilot to more than 100,000 employees, taking the combined commitment to beyond 300,000 in less than six months, the American tech giant said on June 3. The milestone marks one of Microsoft's largest enterprise AI rollouts globally and signals a shift from pilot programmes to organisation-wide adoption of AI-powered workflows, coming despite the growing concerns around rising AI token costs. The expansion follows the 50,000-seat deployments announced by the three companies in December. Microsoft said the latest scale-up reflects how AI is becoming embedded across engineering, service delivery, productivity and business operations functions. According to Microsoft, Infosys has crossed 100,000 Copilot users with monthly active usage exceeding 91 percent. TCS said 86 percent of employees with Copilot licences actively use the tool in their daily work, with some teams reporting productivity improvements of 20-25 percent in research and content creation, twice-faster insight generation, and a 25-35 percent reduction in select work-cycle times. Related Stories Stocks to Watch Today: Dabur India, Sagility, GNG Electronics, Lenskart Solutions, Hexagon Nutrition... Infosys stock rating: CLSA bullish, sees $300-400 billion opportunity in GenAI; check target price Infosys shares fall over 1.5% as stock turns ex-dividend; price adjusts downward Wipro reported over 95 percent monthly active usage. The company said employees generate 7.5 million prompts every month and save more than 250,000 full-time-equivalent workdays every quarter through Copilot-assisted workflows. Wipro has also developed more than 29,000 employee-built AI agents and over 60 enterprise-grade agentic AI solutions. "What Infosys, TCS and Wipro are doing is remarkable. They are putting Copilot at the heart of how their teams work — pairing intelligence with trust, at a scale only true enterprise leaders can deliver,” Puneet Chandok, president, Microsoft India and South Asia. “This is the shift we are seeing with our most ambitious customers: moving from experimenting with AI to building their businesses around it.” The best AI deployments translate an organisation's intelligence into decisions that reshape customer engagement, business processes and growth — and global IT majors are doing exactly that with Microsoft, he said. “It is a powerful example of Intelligence + Trust in action, and of how Frontier Firms turn AI into durable competitive advantage.” Microsoft said the broader adoption aligns with the emergence of what it calls "Frontier Firms"—organisations that redesign work around human-AI collaboration and agentic workflows. Globally, Microsoft now has 20 million paid Copilot seats, with seat additions growing more than 250 percent during the quarter. The next phase of deployment at the three Indian IT firms will focus on extending AI deeper into client delivery, business operations and enterprise workflows, Microsoft said.

Title: Wipro to raise Aggne stake to 80% with $28.5-million buyout
Article: wipro IT giant Wipro Ltd said, on June 1, that it will acquire an additional 20 percent stake in Aggne Global Inc. for $28.5 million, taking its overall shareholding in the insurtech company to 80 percent as the Bengaluru-headquartered IT major sharpens its focus on AI-led transformation in the insurance sector. The transaction, through step-down subsidiary Wipro IT Services, LLC, is expected to close by June 5, according to a stock exchange filing. Aggne operates in the property and casualty insurance segment, offering IT consulting and managed services tailored for insurers. Wipro said the additional stake purchase would strengthen its competitive advantage in the insurance sector, particularly in driving AI, and IP-led transformations for property and casualty insurers. The latest deal follows Wipro’s acquisition of a 60 percent stake in Aggne Global IT Services Pvt Ltd and Aggne Global Inc. in February 2024. Aggne Group, which includes Aggne Global Inc. and Aggne Global IT Services Pvt Ltd, describes itself as an insurtech company focused on AI and intellectual property-driven services aimed at helping insurers modernise operations and improve efficiencies. The company has seen steady growth in recent years, with annual turnover rising from $18.9 million in FY24 to $24.2 million in FY25 and $27.5 million in FY26.

Wipro’s ADR (American Depositary Receipt) surged almost 19% overnight on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) following the partnership announcement with ServiceNow. This is the biggest single-day gain that the US-listed entity has seen since October 2008. Wipro ADR ended at $2.43 with a gain of 18.54% on May 28. At 9:52 am on May 29, Wipro shares were trading 2.2% higher at Rs 206 apiece and Nifty IT index snapped two-day fall to trade nearly 2.5% higher. The sectoral index snapped two-day fall and was trading higher with gains led by Infosys, Wipro, HCLTech, which climbed 4%, 2.2% and 1.8%, respectively. The shares were the top Nifty gainers too along with another IT giant TCS (up 1.76%). Wipro stock has been witnessing steady buying after the company announced the buyback at a price of Rs 250 per share, representing a premium of nearly 21% over its previous closing price. The IT major has fixed June 5 as the record date for the buyback, making shareholders eligible to participate in the programme. Wipro’s board had approved the proposal on April 16 to repurchase up to 60 crore equity shares through the tender offer route. The buyback size represents over 5 percent of the company’s total equity share capital. The Bengaluru-headquartered company said the buyback would be carried out at Rs 250 per share, significantly above the prevailing market price at the time of the announcement. The company had also stated that members of the promoter and promoter group intend to participate in the buyback programme. This marks Wipro’s first share buyback announcement in almost three years. The previous buyback was undertaken in June 2023, when the company repurchased shares worth Rs 12,000 crore between June 22 and June 30. During that programme, Wipro bought back 26.96 crore equity shares, equivalent to 4.91% of its total equity capital, at Rs 445 per share.

Title: Wipro ADRs surge 18% after expanded ServiceNow tie-up; Indian shares in focus ahead of buyback record date
Article: Wipro Ltd Wipro's US shares surged 21 percent after ServiceNow partnership Expanded deal to deploy AI workflows across IT, HR, procurement Wipro's share buyback in India set for June 5, Rs 250 per share Did our AI summary help? W W Watchlist Portfolio Message Follow NSE/BSE Select NSE LIVE BSE LIVE Trade Day High Day Low Volume (NSE) More × Add to Watchlist × Bengaluru-based technology services provider Wipro Ltd.’s US-listed shares jumped as much as 21 percent on Thursday, May 28, after the company announced an expanded partnership with ServiceNow to implement and scale agentic AI workflows across core enterprise functions. Wipro ’s American Depositary Receipt (ADR) rallied 21 percent intraday, settling up 18.54 percent at $2.43 on the New York Stock Exchange. The move marked the biggest single-day gain for the US-listed entity since October 2008. The ADR has now risen in eight of the last 10 trading sessions and gained roughly 31 percent over those 10 sessions. The surge followed Wipro’s announcement that it will integrate Wipro Intelligence -- the company’s unified suite of AI-powered platforms and solutions -- with the ServiceNow AI Platform. The collaboration is aimed at enabling organizations to streamline initiation, orchestration and execution of work across enterprise systems while embedding policy-aligned governance and auditability into workflow design. Share Markets Live Updates | Sensex, Nifty, GIFT Nifty Today Wipro said the expanded partnership will deploy agentic AI workflows across IT, HR, procurement and cybersecurity. Specific use cases include reducing bottlenecks across the procurement value chain, speeding issue resolution and improving service quality in telecom, and strengthening security operations by improving vulnerability and incident response workflows across the cybersecurity value chain. Key benefits cited by Wipro include a unified experience for submitting and managing work requests across enterprise functions; policy-aligned execution with governance and auditability built directly into workflow design; improved cycle times by eliminating manual coordination and unnecessary handoffs; and clearer operational visibility into status, ownership and process completion. The buyback -- which will see Wipro repurchase up to 60 crore equity shares (more than 5 percent of equity) at Rs 250 per share -- is also available to ADR holders. In India, Wipro had seen an eight-day winning streak in its domestic listing before the stock ended lower on Wednesday, May 27, closing at Rs 201.6 on the BSE, down 1.1 percent that day. Over the last month the ADR price has gained about 21 percent, and it has risen roughly 9 percent in three months; however, it remains down 11 percent over six months and 16 percent over one year. Title: Wipro ADR gains 13% as AI partnerships fuel optimism
Article: Improved outlook for technology spending in the US market further added to positive sentiment surrounding Wipro ADR. Wipro ADR surged 13% in US amid optimism on AI initiatives Wipro deepened ServiceNow alliance to roll out agentic AI AI integration targets IT, HR, procurement, and cybersecurity Did our AI summary help? Wipro Ltd’s American Depositary Receipt (ADR) witnessed a sharp rally in the US market, surging over 13% to around $2.33 during intraday trade, amid growing investor optimism around the company’s expanding Artificial Intelligence (AI) and digital business initiatives. The strong upward move in Wipro ADR comes as the IT major continues to strengthen its presence in enterprise AI solutions, automation platforms and digital transformation services. Market participants are increasingly bullish on companies accelerating AI adoption and building large-scale AI-driven workflows for global clients. Investor sentiment also received a boost from Wipro’s recent AI partnerships and strategic focus on integrating generative AI capabilities across industries including banking, healthcare, retail and manufacturing. Analysts believe the company’s aggressive push into AI-led services could support future revenue growth and improve deal momentum. Improved outlook for technology spending in the US market further added to positive sentiment surrounding Wipro ADR. During US trading, Wipro ADR climbed sharply to $2.33, gaining 13.66%, indicating strong buying interest from global investors and signaling potential positive momentum for the stock on Indian exchanges as well. Related Stories Ex-Wipro employee alleges attempt at religious conversion by female colleague in Pune Flawed AI, deepfakes and geopolitical conflicts pose major risks to business: Wipro Wipro CEO Srinivas Pallia’s FY26 pay slips 8% to Rs 49.6 crore An American Depositary Receipt (ADR) is a financial instrument that allows shares of foreign companies to trade on US stock exchanges similar to shares of American companies. ADRs are issued by US banks and represent ownership in shares of overseas companies, enabling global firms like Wipro to access US investors and improve international market participation. Earlier on Thursday, Wipro announced that it has deepened its alliance with ServiceNow to roll out agentic AI across major enterprise functions, in a move aimed at helping large organisations move beyond AI experimentation toward operational deployment at scale. Under the expanded agreement, Wipro will connect its Intelligence platform — a consolidated suite of AI tools — with the ServiceNow AI Platform to reduce manual coordination and improve visibility into how work flows across organisational systems. The integration targets four core enterprise domains: IT, human resources, procurement, and cybersecurity. Three Wipro Intelligence solutions sit at the heart of the arrangement. SmartProcure will automate procurement through standardised intake, approvals, and execution processes. Telco Autonomous Networks will tackle service operations in the telecom sector, pairing AI workflows with industry-specific context. Cyber Transform will focus on security operations, covering vulnerability management and incident response governance. Malay Joshi, chief executive of Wipro's Americas 1 Strategic Market Unit, framed the partnership as a response to a problem widespread among large enterprises: not a shortage of AI ambition, but a persistent difficulty in scaling implementation. The approach, he said, is consulting-led and built to deliver measurable outcomes within defined governance controls. Amit Zavery, president and chief operating officer at ServiceNow, described the direction of travel as toward an "agentic enterprise" — one where AI operates inside secure, governed workflows to produce concrete business results rather than functioning as a standalone tool. For clients, the practical benefits are expected to include faster processing cycles, a single interface for managing work requests, compliance built into workflow design from the outset, and clearer tracking of task ownership and completion across functions.

Title: Competition to Coopetition: IT firms account for nearly 30% of India’s GCC tech workforce
Article: Representative image India GCCs still depend on IT firms for tech talent and expertise Mega GCCs limit outsourcing, mid-sized centers use hybrid models GCCs focus on core IP, AI, and product work, partner for scaling Did our AI summary help? Even as India’s global capability centres (GCCs) continue expanding their in-house technology capabilities, their dependence on IT services firms remains significant, according to industry experts. Rather than replacing outsourcing providers, GCCs are increasingly forming partnerships with them. In fact, nearly 15-30 percent of tech workforce at GCCs are still being outsourced from third party IT service providers, data from staffing firm TeamLease Digital and GCC consulting firm Inductus Group showed. This underscores the growing interest of IT and consulting firms such as Accenture, Cognizant, Tata Consultancy Services, Infosys, HCLTech, Wipro and Tech Mahindra in setting up dedicated GCC units to capture the burgeoning demand. According to a Nasscom-Zinnov report, India currently hosts 2,117 centres across 3,728 units, employing around 2.36 million professionals. About 96 percent of these GCCs were launched post FY21. The ecosystem’s total market size stands at $98.4 billion. Mature GCCs, however, are increasingly retaining 70-75 percent of their technology workforce internally to protect proprietary intellectual property and strengthen core digital engineering capabilities. Related Stories Stocks to Watch Today: Dabur India, Sagility, GNG Electronics, Lenskart Solutions, Hexagon Nutrition... Infosys stock rating: CLSA bullish, sees $300-400 billion opportunity in GenAI; check target price Infosys shares fall over 1.5% as stock turns ex-dividend; price adjusts downward “MNCs that don't yet have a GCC in India outsource only a marginal share of their global workforce here. Once they establish a scaled GCC, outsourcing intensity nearly doubles,” Nitika Goel, managing partner and CMO, Zinnov told Moneycontrol. Among Mega GCCs specifically, roughly one in five roles in their global workforce ecosystem runs through India-based partner networks, as per Zinnov’s data. Mega GCCs refers to captives with over 5,000 professionals. How are IT firms helping GCCs in post AI world? Goel added that GCCs and IT companies are growing symbiotically. The more strategic and capable a GCC becomes, the more it relies on ecosystem partners for scalability, execution bandwidth, and specialised capabilities. The two models are becoming increasingly interdependent. In a previous interview, Barclays Global Services CEO Praveen Kumar told Moneycontrol that the bank outsourced around 30-40 percent of its tech workforce in India from IT partners. Across its multiple GCC locations in the country, the UK-headquartered bank has over 32,000 employees. Neeti Sharma, CEO, TeamLease Digital said that GCCs are still dependent on IT firms for managing and modernizing legacy platforms, migration, cybersecurity operations and managed services. “GCCs are now keeping more core IP, product ownership, architecture, and AI strategy work in-house while leveraging IT Services companies for execution scale and specialised talent,” she said. But, AI has also fundamentally reset that equation. The most mature GCCs are now building ecosystem-led operating models. “Internal teams own enterprise context and strategic direction. Partners provide speed, specialisation, and scalable execution. This is a fundamentally more sophisticated way of operating, and one that is fast becoming the norm across the industry,” said Goel. Large versus mid-sized GCCs These partnership terms also shifts when a large GCC is compared to mid-sized GCCs, which generally have around 100 to 500 employees. According to Alouk Kumar, founder and CEO of Inductus, mega GCCs would typically try to limit their third-party outsourcing to about 10 percent to 15 percent of their total tech operations. For a mid-tier center, collaborating with an established IT service provider or a specialized local vendor is not just a way to cut costs; it is a vital strategy for speeding up their market entry. “This approach allows them to rent specialized skills and navigate the competitive Indian talent market while slowly building up their own capabilities,” he said. Kumar added, “Since these mid-market companies do not have the extensive local HR and legal setups of a Fortune 500 company, they commonly use hybrid or partner-led models, like Build-Operate-Transfer frameworks, to grow quickly without significant upfront financial risk.” For a mid-tier center, collaborating with an established IT service provider or a specialized local vendor is not just a way to cut costs; it is a vital strategy for speeding up their market entry. This approach allows them to rent specialized skills and navigate the competitive Indian talent market while slowly building up their own capabilities. Outcome-driven partnerships Meanwhile, GCCs continue to expand specialised in-house talent. In the current fiscal cycle, GCCs outpaced traditional IT majors by adding roughly 200,000 net new advanced engineering roles, contrasting sharply with the highly measured net addition of approximately 110,000 across third-party IT majors who face tighter organic growth limits, as per data from Inductus. Industry experts said FY27 will be a structural reset in how GCCs and IT services firms work together. The new model will reward scale of capability, platforms, and AI leverage. As GCCs evolve into enterprise nerve centres, the nature of IT services partnerships is shifting meaningfully, experts said. Goel is seeing engagements becoming more “outcome-led,” AI-centric, platform-oriented, and more deeply embedded into enterprise transformation. “The gradual move away from FTE (full time employee)-led contracts toward productivity-linked and transformation-linked engagements is already visible,” she said. While IT majors remain deeply integrated into GCC ecosystems today, competitive differentiation will depend on who can operate as an AI transformation partner, not just a traditional outsourcing vendor, experts said. TeamLease’s Sharma added, “GCCs will continue building core product and AI capabilities internally, but will continue to partner for AI deployment, cloud modernization, cybersecurity, platform engineering, and rapid scaling of niche talent.

Title: Wipro shares rise for 9th straight session, buyback record date at June 5
Article: Wipro shares rise in trade. Wipro shares rose for the ninth straight session on Tuesday amid the company’s Rs 15,000 crore share buyback programme priced at Rs 250 per share, a premium of about 21 percent over the stock’s previous closing price of Rs 206.84 on the NSE. The IT services company has fixed June 5 as the record date for the buyback. This is the company’s first share buyback announcement in nearly three years. The last buyback was undertaken in June 2023, when the company repurchased shares worth Rs 12,000 crore between June 22 and June 30. It bought back 26.96 crore equity shares, representing 4.91 percent of its total equity, at Rs 445 per share. The price is not adjusted for the 1:1 bonus issue announced in December 2024. Title: Wipro founder Azim Premji’s son Tariq buys Rs 45.71-crore penthouse in Bengaluru
Article: Wipro founder Azim Premji’s son Tariq Premji purchases Rs 45.71 crore-luxury penthouse in Bengaluru Tariq Premji buys Bengaluru penthouse for Rs 45.71 crore Penthouse spans 10,602 sq ft in 27 Summit, Sampangiramnagar Deal has 11 basement parking spots and 2,952 sq ft land share Did our AI summary help? Tariq Premji, the younger son of Wipro Ltd founder and billionaire philanthropist Azim Premji, has bought a luxury penthouse in central Bengaluru for Rs 45.71 crore, property registration documents accessed through real estate data analytics firm Zapkey show. The duplex apartment is located in the 27 Summit building in Sampangiramnagar, a premium residential development by Nestled Haven Developers LLP, a subsidiary of Bengaluru-based MAIA Estates. The apartment has a carpet area of 7,065 square feet and a super built-up area of 10,602 square feet, registration documents show. Based on the super built-up area, the transaction values the luxury property at approximately Rs 43,123 per square foot. The deal stands out as one of the largest residential deals in Bengaluru this year, Zapkey co-founder Sandeep Reddy said. The deal also includes 11 covered basement car parking spaces and a proportionate undivided land share of 2,952 square feet, the documents show. There was no immediate response from Tariq Premji or MAIA Estates to Moneycontrol’s  queries. The copy will be updated after receiving a response. Bengaluru housing market Bengaluru’s luxury housing market has continued to witness high-value transactions over the past year, driven by demand from startup founders, technology executives, business families, and high net-worth individuals, market observers said. Central business district locations and premium micro-markets such as Sampangiramnagar, Richmond Town, Indiranagar and Koramangala have seen increased interest for large-format residences and high-end developments. MAIA Estates has emerged as an active player in Bengaluru’s luxury residential segment, focusing on boutique premium projects in core city locations. 27 Summit is a residential development off Richmond Road in Central Bengaluru and spans 2.61 acre. The project has 75 residences ranging from 3 BHK to 4.5 BHK units. “We have seen a steady trickle of high-value transactions registered at 27 Summit in the past, but a ticket size of this magnitude underscores the building's position as a landmark ultra-luxury destination in the Central Business District,” Reddy said. Across the city, the ultra-luxury market is decoupling from broader residential trends; ultra-high-net-worth individuals are increasingly willing to pay a significant premium for unique, 'villa-in-the-sky' concepts that offer absolute privacy, low density, and world class design, he said. Who is Tariq Premji? Tariq is Azim Premji ’s younger son and is associated with family office Azim Premji. The investment arm manages a diverse portfolio of technology, healthcare, consumer, financial services and growth-stage companies. Premji Invest has backed several Indian startups and businesses and is regarded as one of the country’s prominent investment platforms. Azim Premji is among India’s leading business leaders and philanthropists. Through the Azim Premji Foundation, he has committed a significant portion of his wealth towards education and social development initiatives. Tariq completed his graduation in commerce from St Joseph’s College in Bengaluru. In 2018, he was appointed a board member of Wipro Enterprises. He is also a part of the Azim Premji Foundation.

Title: OpenAI’s $4 billion AI bet could challenge TCS, Infosys and Wipro
Article: OpenAI said the venture helps organisations deploy AI in business workflows. OpenAI forms Deployment Company with $4B+ investment Company to help businesses deploy AI in daily operations OpenAI acquires Tomoro, adding 150 engineers to the new venture Did our AI summary help? OpenAI has launched the OpenAI Deployment Company, a new enterprise-focused business backed by more than USD 4 billion in initial investment, marking the ChatGPT maker’s biggest push yet into corporate AI services. The venture will help companies design, build and run artificial intelligence systems across daily operations and is expected to intensify competition in the global technology services market led by firms such as Infosys, Tata Consultancy Services and Wipro. The new company will operate as a separate business unit but remain majority-owned by OpenAI. OpenAI said the venture was created to help organisations move from testing AI tools to deploying them in real business workflows. The company plans to place specialised engineers directly inside client organisations to identify tasks that can be automated or improved using AI systems. As part of the launch, OpenAI has agreed to acquire Tomoro, an AI consulting and engineering firm founded in 2023. The acquisition will bring nearly 150 engineers and deployment specialists into the new company. Tomoro has previously worked with major brands including Tesco, Mattel, Red Bull and Virgin Atlantic. The initiative is backed by 19 investment firms, consultancies and system integrators. The partnership is led by TPG, while Advent, Bain Capital and Brookfield are among the co-lead investors. Other partners include Goldman Sachs, Capgemini and SoftBank Corp. Related Stories Karnataka approves 50 AI labs in govt colleges to boost tech skills, employability Digitized Data, A.I. Agents, High Quality Assets- PNB MD & CEO Reveals The New Face of PSU Banks General Catalyst, India Quotient invest $1.5 million in kids-focussed AI startup Omli OpenAI said these firms collectively support more than 2,000 businesses worldwide, giving the venture access to a large corporate customer base from the start. Industry analysts say the move reflects growing demand from companies seeking practical AI solutions rather than experimental tools. Many businesses are struggling to integrate AI into existing operations because they lack skilled engineers and implementation support. OpenAI’s strategy mirrors the consulting-led model used by companies such as Palantir, where engineers work closely with clients to customise systems for real-world use. The launch also signals rising pressure on traditional IT outsourcing firms. Indian technology companies including Infosys, TCS and Wipro have expanded AI consulting services over the past two years, but OpenAI’s direct entry into enterprise deployment could reshape competition in the rapidly growing AI services market.

Title: HCLTech, Wipro, Tech Mahindra, Persistent recheck harassment safeguards after TCS Nashik case
Article: Representative image India’s biggest IT services companies HCLTech, Wipro, Tech Mahindra and Persistent Systems weighed in on the Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) Nashik sexual harassment case by revisiting their Prevention of Sexual Harassment (POSH) compliance audits. IT companies opened up about their internal checks and balances pertaining to workplace harassments during the fourth quarter earnings season earlier this month, as public scrutiny in the TCS case intensified, as allegations of sexual harassment and attempts to hurt religious sentiments surfaced against some of the employees in the company's Nashik BPO unit. Safeguards at HCLTech HCLTech’s chief people officer Ram Sundararajan called the incident “very unfortunate.” “I don't think anybody sitting in any management team will ever turn their eyes away from such a situation. We do take it very seriously,” he said in a packed room during the company’s earnings conference on April 21. Related Stories TCS will take strict action if any associate is found responsible: N Chandrasekaran on Nashik case TCS AI revenue nears $2.5 billion annualised; AI agents could match employee count: N Chandrasekaran TCS will not be hiring the kind of numbers it used to hire: Chairman N Chandrasekaran He added that just like any other large enterprise, HCLTech too has a robust POSH process, while the company additionally conducts independent adequacy, compliance, effectiveness (ACE) audits . These independent audits then get reported at the highest level of management. “It's one thing to have the right policy, set out the right procedures, build the right channels, create awareness, and make sure that those processes work. But it's also important to get it independently audited and verified to see whether what we have is adequate,” Sundararajan explained. The TCS case allegedly saw internal verbal complaints being raised by the complainants between 2022 and 2026 to their reporting managers, yet no action was taken by them. The Wipro way Rival Wipro said it has kept its POSH committee independent that doesn’t report to any individual within the organisation. The committee reports to an independent audit committee rather than the HR function. Wipro’s chief human resources officer Saurabh Govil said POSH compliance is reviewed closely every quarter, signalling tighter oversight at the board level. “We review POSH compliance closely every quarter,” he said, reiterating that the company believes it has “robust mechanisms” in place. Checks at Tech Mahindra Tech Mahindra CEO and MD Mohit Joshi stated that the company has not seen any such instances that “breaches any internal codes of conduct.” “We are very comfortable with the strength of our processes, the level of board oversight, the level of HR checks and controls and quality of the revisions that we have, and also about the strength of our internal auditing,” Joshi told Moneycontrol during the company’s earnings conference in Pune last week. He added, “Obviously, we want to stay very focused on employee welfare. We have always been looking at our processes and seeing where they can be strengthened or where operational controls can be improved.” Mock trainings at Persistent Systems Meanwhile, Persistent Systems said it conducts routine mock trainings with employees regarding POSH processes. Chief Financial Officer Vinit Teredesai told Moneycontrol that the company keeps challenging its “own ethical processes and POSH committee to ensure complaints are addressed and solution is reached at a much faster pace than normal industry standards.” “We continue to keep investing more in this area by increasing awareness, increasing education, building in security as well as ensuring that any untowardly incident doesn't happen. There is nothing called as foolproof, but obviously our job is to ensure that we create an environment whereby an employee feels comfortable in approaching the management without any fear in their mind,” he said. What happened in the TCS case? The case came to light earlier in April. Around nine complainants, including eight females and a male employee of the Nashik facility filed First Information Report (FIRs) against eight accused, alleging several incidents of mental and sexual harassment at workplace, hurting religious sentiments and in one complaint, religious coercion by colleagues. Across these complaints, the key accused include Danish Sheikh (named in two FIRs), Tausif Attar (six FIRs), Shafi Shaikh (four FIRs), Raza Memon (five FIRs), Shahrukh Qureshi (four FIRs), Asif Ansari (two FIRs), Nida Khan (one FIR) and Ashwini Chainani (one FIR). Also read: TCS Nashik row: Inside the FIR trail and how a small unit drew outsized scrutiny What did TCS management say? Following the allegations, TCS ordered an internal probe to look into the complaints and why it didn’t get reported. Tata Sons chairman N Chandrasekaran said the Tata Group maintains a zero-tolerance policy towards any form of coercion or misconduct by its employees. He added that “a thorough investigation is underway to establish the facts and identify all individuals responsible for this situation,” stressing that “appropriate and stringent action will be taken against those found guilty.” Addressing the specific case, Chandrasekaran said, “Complaints and allegations emerging from the Nashik branch of TCS have been gravely concerning and deeply anguishing.” TCS CEO and MD K Krithivasan later shared that the company didn’t receive any complaints regarding these incidents through its formal POSH channels. TCS has hired Deloitte and Trilegal as independent advisors to support its internal investigation, which is being overseen by Aarthi Subramanian, President and Chief Operating Officer of TCS. The company has also constituted an Oversight Committee, chaired by independent director Keki Mistry, to review findings and recommend further action.

Title: AI rewrites playbook for TCS, Infosys, Wipro as growth outlook splits: Report
Article: This paradigm shift is starkly reflected in mixed FY27 outlooks and evolving talent metrics. (Representative Photo) Indian IT firms hit by AI disruption, global economic strain TCS, Infosys profits rise; HCLTech, Wipro face uncertainty AI-led deals offset revenue decline in traditional IT services Did our AI summary help? India’s top IT firms — TCS, Infosys, HCLTech, Wipro, and Tech Mahindra — closed FY26 at a turning point, navigating global economic pressures, West Asian geopolitical tensions, and the rapid rise of Artificial Intelligence (AI), according to a PTI report. The sector is steadily moving away from traditional effort-based services, as AI-driven productivity begins to compress revenues in legacy businesses. However, this pressure is being partly offset by a surge in large AI-led deals, pushing companies to shift from scale-based contracts to more outcome-driven, modular engagements. This transition is reflected in mixed outlooks for FY27. While TCS and Infosys indicated easing macro headwinds, HCLTech and Wipro flagged continued uncertainty and weak discretionary spending. A report by ICICI Direct noted, "AI may cause about 2-3 per cent annual deflation in traditional IT services revenues for the next couple of years. Indian IT services could see an incremental AI-led TAM of USD 300-400 billion by 2030." TCS reported a 12.22 per cent rise in March quarter net profit to Rs 13,718 crore, with revenue growing 9.64 per cent to Rs 70,698 crore. For FY26, profit rose 1.35 per cent to Rs 49,210 crore, while revenue increased 4.58 per cent to Rs 2.67 lakh crore. The company said it is entering the new fiscal year with positive momentum, adding that most recent headwinds are behind it. Its annualised AI services revenue crossed USD 2.3 billion, accounting for over 6 per cent of total revenue. Related Stories Stocks to Watch Today: Dabur India, Sagility, GNG Electronics, Lenskart Solutions, Hexagon Nutrition... Infosys stock rating: CLSA bullish, sees $300-400 billion opportunity in GenAI; check target price Infosys shares fall over 1.5% as stock turns ex-dividend; price adjusts downward TCS CEO K Krithivasan said the company is entering the new fiscal year with positive momentum driven by deal wins and expects limited impact from the West Asia crisis, largely confined to clients in travel, transportation, and the Gulf region. Infosys posted a 20.8 per cent jump in consolidated net profit to Rs 8,501 crore in the March quarter, with revenue rising 13.4 per cent to Rs 46,402 crore. For FY26, profit grew 10.20 per cent to Rs 29,440 crore and revenue rose 9.6 per cent to Rs 178,650 crore. The company acknowledged that AI is beginning to cannibalise traditional services, though growth in AI-led offerings is offsetting the impact. "With the Iran war, there was a change in the economic environment, but there seem to be paths towards things stabilising. What we understand (through) talking to people in the market and the clients is that the underlying resilience of some of the economies where we have big markets is pretty good. The economies are doing well. There are good investments. AI is growing well," Infosys CEO Salil Parekh said. HCLTech reported a 4.2 per cent rise in Q4 net profit to Rs 4,488 crore, with revenue up 12.34 per cent to Rs 33,981 crore. For FY26, profit declined 4.30 per cent to Rs 16,642 crore, while revenue increased 11.18 per cent to Rs 130,144 crore. The company projected FY27 growth of 1-4 per cent in constant currency, citing continued volatility. "During the quarter, our performance came below our expectations due to softness in certain parts of our business, due to lower discretionary spend and delayed decision-making. "Our new AI-led service offerings are getting traction in the market and are reflected in annualised Advanced AI revenues crossing USD 620 million in Q4. Our #1 priority in FY27 is to ensure the company is positioned right to take advantage of AI opportunities for multi-decade value creation," said HCLTech CEO C Vijayakumar. Wipro reported a 1.89 per cent decline in Q4 net profit to Rs 3,501.8 crore, while revenue rose 7.6 per cent to Rs 24,236.3 crore. For FY26, net profit rose marginally by 0.47 per cent to Rs 13,197.4 crore, with revenue at Rs 92,624 crore. The company described the current macro environment as the “new normal”, shaped by geopolitical and policy disruptions, and approved a Rs 15,000 crore share buyback. Tech Mahindra posted a 16 per cent increase in Q4 net profit to Rs 1,353.8 crore, with revenue up 12.6 per cent to Rs 15,076.1 crore. For FY26, profit rose 13.15 per cent to Rs 4,810.9 crore, while revenue grew 7.2 per cent to Rs 56,815.4 crore. The company said AI presents a significant medium- to long-term opportunity for client transformation rather than a risk to revenues. *With Agency Inputs

Title: TCS, Infosys, HCLTech, Wipro, Tech M report muted FY26 hiring; workforce shrinks by 7,389
Article: Representative image Financial year 2026 showed a clear shift in India’s IT hiring narrative, with the country’s top firms turning cautious on workforce expansion as they restructure business operations around their artificial intelligence (AI) goals. Overall headcount addition across Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), Infosys, HCLTech, Wipro and Tech Mahindra plunged sharply, falling by 7,389 in FY26, reversing the modest gains of 12,718 seen in FY25. The biggest shocker for the IT industry in FY26 was the country's largest private sector employer TCS deciding to lay off 12,000 people from its workforce. This was the biggest job cut by an Indian corporate entity in recent times. This had led to the overall hiring of the top five IT companies to slide to negative, though three out of five firms have reported net positive headcount addition. Management commentary across the board have continued to signal caution when it came to hiring. Companies shied away from sharing definitive outlook on hiring plans for FY27. Individually, in Q4FY26, Infosys’ headcount decreased by 8,440 employees while TCS added 2,356, HCLTech added 802 employees and Wipro’s headcount marginally increased by 135 sequentially. Tech Mahindra lost around 1,993 employees. Related Stories Stocks to Watch Today: Dabur India, Sagility, GNG Electronics, Lenskart Solutions, Hexagon Nutrition... Infosys stock rating: CLSA bullish, sees $300-400 billion opportunity in GenAI; check target price Infosys shares fall over 1.5% as stock turns ex-dividend; price adjusts downward According to Nasscom’s latest annual strategic review, India’s technology industry added only about 2,000 more net jobs year-on-year in FY26. Net additions stood at roughly 1.35 lakh employees, only marginally higher than the approximately 1.33 lakh jobs added in FY25, indicating that hiring growth has largely plateaued. At an industry level, the sector’s total workforce grew to 59.5 lakh (5.95 million) employees in FY26, up from 58.2 lakh (5.82 million) in the previous year, translating into a 2.3 percent increase in headcount. Fresher hiring targets TCS said it will hire 25,000 freshers in FY27 as of now, and will increase target depending on the demand during the year. This is significantly lower than its previous fresher addition of 40,000-42,000 in FY25 and FY26. Infosys plans to hire for a similar fresher number of 20,000 in the new fiscal year, as it did in FY26. HCLTech, Tech Mahindra and Wipro said they will decide as the new fiscal year progresses. In FY26, HCLTech added 11,744 freshers and Wipro added 7,500 employees. What did the company managements say? TCS said it is focused on building a “future ready organization by strategically hiring both fresh graduates and experienced professionals. “Our recruitment efforts have been concentrated on individual with expertise in AI, data, enterprise solutions, software engineering, cloud, cybersecurity and digital engineering. In FY2026 we also hired over 750 employees with deep advisory and consulting expertise. We stepped up our investments in talent development initiatives as well,” said Sudeep Kunnumal, CHRO, TCS during the company’s earnings conference on April 9. Infosys CFO Jayesh Sanghrajka said the headcount decline was due to quarterly seasonality. “Our headcount sequentially has gone down, but on a YoY basis it has gone up by 5,000 people. There’s always some quarterly seasonality. Headcount is a function of utilisation and the volumes we see. This quarter the volumes were softer, plus the freshers in the system had to be accounted for,” he said. Rationalising headcount and improving utilisation rate of the existing employees is the backbone of Tech Mahindra’s strategy to improve margins under its three-year business transformation plan. “We had a hired significant number of freshers in the first year of the our plan. As we get into the second year, there was a change in strategy. We said we will take a pause. There was a change demand profile with AI also. This year was more adjustment of that with minimum fresher hiring. As we get into the next year, I think we will resume some of that going forward,” said Rohit Anand, CFO, Tech Mahindra. He added, “But we have not really clearly outlined the number. It won't be as much as the first year. It will definitely be better than what we did last year, which will be in the middle somewhere.” In FY27, HCLTech will be hiring similar number of freshers as it did this year, but the company didn’t state an exact number of headcount to add as it gets evaluated on a quarterly basis. Ram Sundararajan, Chief People Ofiicer, HCLTech said, “Overall hiring is always going to be a variable number. It's very difficult to give an annual forecast for that. Generally, it works on the basis of quarterly and monthly run rates. Entry level hiring, it's going to be on similar lines as what we have done for this year. In the current environment, trying to put out a number on a full year basis, is not going to be a the wise thing to do.” “We don't have any target for fresher hiring for next fiscal. It's completely on demand, very volatile environment,” Wipro’s Chief Human Resource Officer Saurabh Govil said.

The OFS will open on June 9 for non-retail investors and on June 10 for retail investors. IRB Infrastructure Developers The company's toll revenue increased sharply by 25 percent year-on-year to Rs 843 crore in May, compared to Rs 672 crore in the corresponding month last year. Panacea Biotec The company announced the launch of the DENSTAR project, which aims to advance the licensure of its dengue vaccine, DengiAll, in sub-Saharan Africa (sSA) and facilitate its broader global adoption. The four-year initiative is funded under the Global Health European & Developing Countries Clinical Trials Partnership 3 Joint Undertaking (GH EDCTP3 JU), supported by the European Union. Grasim Industries The company announced an investment of Rs 3,094 crore for the Phase II expansion of its Lyocell capacity by 110 KTPA at Harihar, Karnataka. The expansion will comprise two production lines of 55 KTPA (150 tonnes per day) each. The first line is expected to be commissioned by 2028, while the second line is expected to be commissioned by 2030. HCL Technologies The company launched the AI Innovation Zone in collaboration with Google Cloud. Located in California, the AI Innovation Zone will enable global enterprises to scale AI applications across agentic, kinetic, and physical AI. Rail Vikas Nigam The company has received a Letter of Acceptance for an EPC order worth Rs 221.33 crore from South East Central Railway. JNK India The company has received a large order worth between Rs 100 crore and Rs 300 crore from CC7 Emirates Engineering Solutions L.L.C., UAE, for waste gas handling systems. JSW Energy The company has commissioned its wind blade manufacturing plant at Halol, Gujarat, marking a significant step towards vertically integrating its wind energy value chain and de-risking its supply chain. Bharti Airtel, Vodafone Idea The Bombay High Court has struck down the Centre's one-time spectrum charge imposed on Bharti Airtel and Vodafone Idea, ruling that the government lacked the authority to retrospectively alter the financial terms of telecom licences years after they had been granted. The transaction was executed at Rs 842.5 per share. Iroha Emerging India Fund-I, Arohi Emerging Asia Master Fund, and Arohi Emerging India Master Fund, all managed by Arohi Asset Management, acquired the 43.7 lakh shares. Title: Buy HCL Technologies; target of Rs 1560: LKP Research
Article: Buy HCL02 HCL02 Watchlist Portfolio Message Follow NSE/BSE Select NSE LIVE BSE LIVE Trade Day High Day Low Volume (NSE) More × Add to Watchlist × LKP Research's research report on HCL Technologies HCL Technologies Ltd (HCL) reported a soft FY26 performance alongside a muted quarterly print, highlighting persistent demand-side pressures and margin volatility owing to restructuring costs. Q4 FY26 revenue stood at USD 3.68bn, declining 3.3% QoQ while growing 2.4% YoY in constant currency (CC) terms, impacted by continued weakness in discretionary spending across key verticals, cautious client behavior, and slower deal rampups. EBIT margins contracted 200bps QoQ to 16.5%, below expectations, driven by an unfavorable revenue mix, restructuring costs and ongoing investments in capabilities. Outlook Factoring in the combined impact of AI-led disruption, sustained weakness in discretionary spending, deflationary pressures from GenAI, and heightened geopolitical uncertainties, we moderate our growth and margin assumptions, leading to a valuation cut with a revised TP of ₹1,560, based on 20x FY28E EPS, while maintaining our BUY rating on HCL. Title: Reduce HCL Technologies; target of Rs 1300: Prabhudas Lilladher
Article: Reduce HCL02 HCL02 Watchlist Portfolio Message Follow NSE/BSE Select NSE LIVE BSE LIVE Trade Day High Day Low Volume (NSE) More × Add to Watchlist × Prabhudas Lilladher's research report on HCL Technologies HCLT’s Q4 performance was significantly below expectations, with revenue declining 3.3% QoQ CC versus our estimate of –2.0% and consensus of –1.6%. The miss was driven by weakness in the Services business, impacted by cancellation of discretionary programs in select telecom clients and termination of two SAP modernization engagements, which are expected to weigh on FY27E revenue by ~50 bps. Beyond these specific headwinds, subdued discretionary spending amid geopolitical uncertainty, the near-term deflationary impact of AI, and continued softness in the Software segment are likely to weigh on growth. Consequently, FY27E organic revenue guidance of 1–4% and Services revenue guidance of 1.5–4.5% point to a weaker demand environment, with the upper end contingent on a recovery in macro conditions. Accordingly, we revise our revenue growth estimates downward to 2.6% and 3.8% YoY CC for FY27E and FY28E, respectively (vs. earlier 6.5% and 7.1%). On margins front, while FY26 restructuring impacts (~70 bps) are now behind us, ongoing investments in capability building are expected to keep margins under pressure; we therefore lower our EBIT margin estimates to 17.7% for FY27E and 18.0% for FY28E (vs. earlier 18.0% and 18.3%). Outlook We now factor in a USD revenue CAGR of 3.3% and INR EPS CAGR of 6.8% over FY26–28E; consequently, we cut our target PE multiple to 17x (from 20x) on FY28E earnings, leading to a revised target price of INR 1,300 (earlier INR 1,710), and downgrade the stock to REDUCE from BUY. Title: HCL Tech stock crashes over 10% after Q4 miss, weak FY27 guidance; brokerages cut target prices
Article: HCL Tech HCL Tech shares fell over 10% after weak Q4 results and guidance Q4 profit and revenue missed analyst estimates Brokerages cut target prices, flag weak near-term growth Did our AI summary help? HCL Technologies stock fell sharply on Wednesday after the company reported weaker-than-expected Q4 results and issued subdued growth guidance for FY27. Shares of HCL Technologies Ltd were trading at Rs 1,292 at noon, down 10.3 percent on the NSE, emerging as the top loser on the Nifty. Brokerages broadly turned cautious on the outlook, cutting target prices and flagging weak near-term growth visibility following misses across key financial metrics. The HCL Tech stock has declined about 11 percent over the past one year, compared with a 1.1 percent gain in the Nifty 50. The IT major reported net profit of Rs 4,488 crore for Q4FY26, up 10.1 percent sequentially and 4.2 percent year-on-year, but below the CNBC-TV18 poll estimate of Rs 4,696 crore. Revenue stood at Rs 33,981 crore, rising 0.3 percent quarter-on-quarter and 12.4 percent year-on-year, also missing expectations. In dollar terms, revenue came in at $3.68 billion versus estimates of $3.76 billion. Share Markets Live Updates | Sensex, Nifty Today Operating performance also disappointed, with EBIT falling 10.6 percent sequentially to Rs 5,620 crore, while margins contracted to 16.5 percent from 18.6 percent in the previous quarter, missing estimates of 17.6 percent. Constant currency revenue declined 3.3 percent sequentially, reflecting continued weakness in the software business, where revenue dropped sharply during the quarter. Related Stories Motilal Oswal Financial Services stock jumps 5% as UBS initiates coverage with ‘buy’; sees 38% u... SBI stock rating: Jefferies bullish, eyes loan repricing for NIM stability; check target price, upsi... Citi cuts Nifty target to 26,000 on geopolitical risks; sees 12 percent upside from current levels Management attributed the miss to lower discretionary spending and delays in decision-making, particularly in telecom, along with cancellations of certain SAP programmes. While IT and business services and ER&D segments saw modest growth, a sharp decline in software revenue weighed on overall performance. The company reported total contract value of $1.94 billion for the quarter, while attrition stood at 12.5 percent. The outlook further dampened sentiment, with HCL Tech guiding for FY27 revenue growth of 1-4 percent in constant currency, below Street expectations. Services growth is expected at 1.5-4.5 percent, while EBIT margin is guided at 17.5-18.5 percent. Management said its focus remains on positioning the company for AI-led opportunities, with annualised advanced AI revenues crossing $620 million. Brokerages cautious on HCL Tech; cut target prices, trim earnings estimates HSBC retained a ‘Hold’ rating but cut its target price to Rs 1,480 from Rs 1,560, citing a sharp Q4 miss and weaker FY27 growth outlook. The brokerage highlighted budget cuts at top US telecom clients and SAP project cancellations as key drags. It added that earnings growth and return ratios are unlikely to compound in double digits in the near term. JPMorgan maintained a ‘Neutral’ rating and lowered its target to Rs 1,370 from Rs 1,419, highlighting revenue, margin and EPS misses. The brokerage warned that weakness in telecom and SAP-related demand could persist into FY27. Citi also maintained a ‘Neutral’ stance with a reduced target of Rs 1,385 , pointing to weak revenue growth, deal momentum and guidance. It said that discretionary spending cuts in telecom and SAP cancellations affected performance. The brokerage added that while near-term outlook remains weak, the company may be relatively better placed over the medium term. Nomura remained constructive with a ‘Buy’ rating but cut its target price to Rs 1,600 from Rs 1,700. It cited an all-round miss and weaker growth outlook, along with EPS downgrades of 5-7 percent for FY27-28. CLSA maintained an ‘Outperform’ rating with a target of Rs 1,519 , but flagged disappointing execution, weak order book and limited visibility on offsetting AI-led deflation. Title: FIIs dump Rs 10,956 cr in IT stocks in early February; holdings at 4-year low
Article: IT stocks FIIs sold over Rs 10,956 crore in IT stocks in early February IT stocks hit by AI concerns, Nifty IT index down 14 percent FIIs bought capital goods, metals, and financial services stocks Did our AI summary help? Foreign institutional investors (FIIs) sold over Rs 10,956 crore in information technology stocks during the first half of February, as the sector witnessed a sharp selloff amid concerns that rapid advances in generative artificial intelligence could reduce demand for large-scale, human-led coding and IT support services, a key revenue driver for India’s software exporters. With this selloff, FIIs’ combined investment in IT stocks declined to Rs 4.49 lakh crore as of February 15, the lowest level in four years, down 16 percent from Rs 5.34 lakh crore at the end of January 2026, according to NSDL data. FIIs have been selling IT stocks continuously since the start of 2025, with total selling of nearly Rs 74,698 crore during the period. Details of stock-wise stake reductions are expected to be released in the March quarter shareholding patterns. FII holdings in IT stocks stood at a record of around Rs 7.3 lakh crore at the start of 2025. With the recent decline in stock prices along with continued selling, the value of their holdings has fallen 38 percent. Related Stories India VIX slumps to five-month low as easing global risks lift market sentiment Sensex, Nifty jump 2%: Market rally adds nearly Rs 10 lakh crore to investor wealth Asian markets rally as Iran deal hopes lift risk appetite, oil prices ease So far in February, Infosys has declined 16.5 percent, TCS has fallen 14 percent and HCL Technologies has dropped 14.2 percent. Tech Mahindra is down 12 percent, while Persistent Systems and Wipro have fallen around 10 percent each. During February to date, the Nifty IT index has declined 14 percent. Meanwhile, mutual funds’ combined investment in the top 10 IT stocks fell to Rs 3.04 lakh crore as of February 13, compared with Rs 3.56 lakh crore at the end of January 2026, indicating a notional loss of over Rs 50,000 crore. According to a JP Morgan report titled “Looking Through the AI Fog,” the sector is likely in the third year of below-par growth during CY23-25, reflecting a combination of structural and cyclical factors. The report said it is difficult to quantify the precise impact of emerging technologies and the duration of the current demand phase. It added that enterprise software development and integration would continue to require significant IT services support. The report further stated that its reverse discounted cash flow analysis suggests stocks are factoring in 4 percent terminal growth at current prices with no short-term acceleration. If the below-par growth seen during CY23-25 were to become permanent terminal growth, the downside to current prices would be at most 10 percent. A scenario of more than 30 percent downside would arise only if companies see zero terminal growth with no growth thereafter. It added that free cash flow and dividend yields are at levels previously seen during major market dislocations such as the global financial crisis and the COVID period. Capital Goods, Metals Attract FII Inflows While FIIs continued to sell IT stocks, they remained buyers in several other sectors. Capital goods stocks saw buying worth Rs 8,032 crore during the first half of February after buying Rs 2,761 crore in January. Financial services and oil and gas stocks recorded buying of Rs 6,175 crore and Rs 4,678 crore, respectively, during the first half of February, after selling over Rs 8,592 crore in financial services and Rs 940 crore in oil and gas in January. Other sectors such as metals and mining, power and construction also saw buying by FIIs. Metals and mining recorded buying worth Rs 3,279 crore during the first half after buying over Rs 11,526 crore in January. Power and construction saw buying worth Rs 3,272 crore and Rs 1,745 crore, respectively, during the first half, after selling over Rs 1,867 crore and Rs 1,532 crore in January. Apart from IT, FIIs were net sellers in FMCG and healthcare stocks. They sold Rs 1,182 crore in FMCG and Rs 1,051 crore in healthcare during the first half of February. In January, FIIs had sold Rs 7,497 crore in FMCG and Rs 6,162 crore in healthcare sectors.

Title: Chartist Talk: Nifty to open directly in 25,600–25,700 resistance zone today; Milan Vaishnav bets on 3 India–US deal-linked stocks
Article: Milan Vaishnav is the MSTA, Founder of Gemstone Equity Research & Advisory Services Nifty needs to eventually cross and stay above 25,850 over the coming days for further rally Bank Nifty may hit 60,000 but likely to slightly underperform Nifty 50 Did our AI summary help? HCL02 AF21 BFC HCL02 Watchlist Portfolio Message Follow NSE/BSE Select NSE LIVE BSE LIVE Trade Day High Day Low Volume (NSE) More AF21 Watchlist Portfolio Message Follow NSE/BSE Select NSE LIVE BSE LIVE Trade Day High Day Low Volume (NSE) More BFC Watchlist Portfolio Message Follow NSE/BSE Select NSE LIVE BSE LIVE Trade Day High Day Low Volume (NSE) More × Add to Watchlist × "We can expect the Nifty to open directly into the 25,600-25,700 resistance zone but it would be important to see that the gap up opening is sustained," said Milan Vaishnav, the MSTA, Founder of Gemstone Equity Research & Advisory Services in an interview to Moneycontrol . Further to that, it would be crucial for the markets to go back inside the original 500-point trading zone created between 26,200 and 25,700. If this happens, then the primary trend would stay intact, he feels. After the US-India trade deal, he advised buying Bharat Forge, Avanti Feeds, and HCL Technologies. Will the Nifty 50 open with a strong gap-up around the 25,600–25,700 zone, which is a crucial resistance area, and could this bring strong momentum to the index? Yes, a gap-up is expected today; we can expect the Nifty to open directly into the 25,600-25,700 resistance zone. Now, there are two things to it. First, it would be important to see that the gap up opening is sustained. Markets will need to capitalize on the strong impetus that it has from the long-awaited trade deal. Further to that, it would be crucial for the markets to go back inside the original 500-point trading zone created between 26,200 and 25,700. If this happens, then the primary trend would stay intact. What key Nifty levels should investors watch, and what is your trading strategy? I would like to point out here that despite the Budget day volatility and the negative close at 24,825, the Nifty had defended the 50-week MA, which is placed at 24,842. This meant that despite the negative move, the primary uptrend was not disrupted. It would have been disrupted if the zone of 24,800-24,300 had been violated. The crucial levels Investors need to watch are Nifty's ability to eventually cross above 25,850 and stay above it over the coming days. So far as trading strategy goes, I would recommend allowing the markets to digest the developments in the first hour of the trade; only once this happens, we can look at fresh buying. We have recommended holding on to existing investments on Budget day also despite the negative move and would continue doing so today as well. Do you expect a sharp spurt in the Nifty IT index, which has been in consolidation mode for a long time? The IT Index is inside the leading quadrant of the Relative Rotation Graph (RRG). It has outperformed the broader Index and is likely to continue to do so. A spurt is also expected to be higher, which is expected to take it past 40,000 levels eventually. Do you expect the Bank Nifty to hit the 60,000 mark? Yes, if we look at a proportionate gap-up opening, this level may be tested. However, it is likely to slightly underperform the Nifty. Which three stocks would you prefer to buy following the India–US trade deal, and why? Bharat Forge The stock may likely see a breakout today. The target may be placed around Rs 1,650 and a protective stop-loss should be placed at Rs 1,350. The company is a direct beneficiary of lower tariffs and improved access to the US auto and industrial ecosystem. It is already deeply embedded in US OEM and defence supply chains, so incremental gains don’t require capacity reinvention. Avanti Feeds The stock is consolidating above its important cluster of support zones. Momentum indicators are favourable. We can expect a target of Rs 900 with a protective stop-loss placed at Rs 750. Positives are the shrimp and seafood exports to the US that would benefit meaningfully from lower trade friction and better pricing power. HCL Technologies The stock is attempting a breakout from a large consolidation pattern. The momentum indicators remain supportive, and the lead indicators are showing mild bullish divergences. A target can be placed near Rs 1,775 with a protective stop-loss at Rs 1,600. The US drives a large share of revenues for HCL Tech from digital transformation, cloud, and infrastructure projects What is your reading of the gold and silver charts? Both Gold and Silver have entered a consolidation phase. Volatility will persist, and the consolidation range may remain wider. While these levels may be used to make a fresh entry, it should be made in small tranches rather than a lump sum. Do you expect the rupee to strengthen against the US dollar based on chart patterns? While USD will likely remain in an uptrend, a mean-reversion in USDINR is likely. However, the dollar may continue to find support in the 90-90.25 zone, and further gains are unlikely.
